title: CanisterSprawl Supply Chain Campaign Uses Decentralized ICP Canister for Resilient C2 Infrastructure
description: The CanisterSprawl supply chain campaign tracked by Socket and StepSecurity used an Internet Computer Protocol canister as a decentralized and resilient C2 channel, making takedowns significantly harder than traditional centralized C2 infrastructure and enabling persistent campaign operations.
label: AV-410

title: BufferZoneCorp Malicious Go Modules Tamper with GitHub Actions Workflows via GITHUB_ENV Manipulation
description: Malicious Go modules in the BufferZoneCorp campaign executed through init() to detect GITHUB_ENV and GITHUB_PATH variables, manipulating workflow environment to intercept subsequent build steps and establish persistent unauthorized access to CI pipeline execution.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious RubyGems Exploit extconf.rb Native Extension Build Process for Install-Time Credential Theft
description: Malicious RubyGems exploited Ruby's native extension build process by weaponizing extconf.rb to execute credential-stealing code automatically during gem installation, running with elevated build permissions before any user invokes the package's advertised functionality.
label: AV-410

title: Automated Upgrade Bots Silently Merge Malicious Package Updates into Protected Branches Within Minutes
description: Supply chain attacks leveraging automated dependency upgrade bots such as Dependabot and Renovate were documented where malicious package updates were silently merged into protected branches within minutes of publication, with automation tools bypassing human review processes intended to catch supply chain compromises.
label: AV-410

title: S1ngularity Attack Bypasses npm Provenance Signing by Publishing Directly with Stolen Token
description: The S1ngularity supply chain attack against Nx bypassed npm provenance signing protections by using a stolen npm publish token to upload malicious packages directly, demonstrating that provenance signing does not prevent installation of unsigned packages published with compromised credentials.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious RubyGem Logger Runtime Path Steals Environment Data on First Logger Invocation
description: Malicious RubyGems implemented a runtime credential theft path wired into the custom logger class that sent environment data including tokens, API keys, and secrets to attacker infrastructure the first time the logger was invoked, combining install-time and runtime attack vectors.
label: AV-410

title: BufferZoneCorp Campaign Uses Sleeper Packages That Activate Malicious Payloads Only After Update
description: The BufferZoneCorp supply chain campaign published initial sleeper versions of Ruby gems and Go modules with plausible utility branding and clean behavior, then updated nearly every package to add active payloads including credential exfiltration, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence.
label: AV-410

title: npm Supply Chain Attack Achieves Cryptocurrency Wallet Drain Within 16 Minutes of Malicious Package Publication
description: A targeted npm supply chain attack documented by Sygnia achieved cryptocurrency wallet draining through traffic interception and transaction manipulation of browser-based crypto wallets within just 16 minutes of publishing malicious updates to widely-used packages.
label: AV-410

title: MavenGate Attack Hijacks Java and Android Dependencies via Expired Domain Purchase Affecting 18% of Maven Central
description: The MavenGate supply chain attack allowed attackers to hijack Java and Android library dependencies by purchasing expired domains corresponding to abandoned Maven groupIds, enabling injection of malicious code into applications built by Google, Facebook, Signal, and Amazon with 6,170 of 33,938 analyzed domains found vulnerable.
label: AV-509

title: MavenGate Enables Malicious Plugin Injection into Java Build Process via Abandoned GroupId Takeover
description: The MavenGate attack demonstrated that beyond dependency hijacking, attackers who gain control of an abandoned Maven groupId can inject malicious Gradle and Maven build plugins that execute arbitrary code during the build process of all applications using the compromised groupId.
label: AV-509

title: Java-Class-Hijack Attack Injects Malicious Class via Maven Dependency Packaging Order to Control Database Logic
description: The Java-Class-Hijack supply chain attack exploits Maven's depth-first dependency packaging order and JVM classpath resolution to inject a malicious class with the same fully qualified name as a legitimate one, demonstrated by compromising the Corona-Warn-App's database connection logic.
label: AV-509

title: Maven-Hijack Exploits Dependency Resolution Order to Override Core Application Behavior Without Modifying Source
description: Maven-Hijack attacks exploit Maven's depth-first search dependency packaging and Java's classpath resolution mechanism to silently override core application behavior by injecting malicious classes into transitive dependencies without modifying main codebase or library names.
label: AV-509

title: Artifactory Maven Proxy Artifact Poisoning via CVE-2024-6915 Allows Serving Malicious spring-boot-starter-web
description: CVE-2024-6915 in JFrog Artifactory allowed attackers to poison Maven proxy repositories by saving any HTTP response from a remote endpoint as an arbitrary artifact, enabling malicious versions of commonly used libraries like spring-boot-starter-web to be served to downstream builds.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Supply Chain Attack Uses Stolen Browser Cookies to Take Over Developer Accounts and Commit Malicious Code
description: A GitHub supply chain attack leveraged stolen browser cookies to hijack developer accounts, using compromised accounts to contribute malicious code with verified commits and publish malicious packages to PyPI linked to popular GitHub projects via a fake pypihosted.org mirror.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Supply Chain Attack Installs Keylogger Capturing Passwords Personal Messages and Financial Details
description: A complex GitHub supply chain attack installed keylogger malware on developer systems that logged all keystrokes including passwords, personal messages, and financial details, while also stealing browser cookies, autofill data, credit cards, and login credentials from Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Yandex.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Decrypts and Steals Discord Tokens to Hijack Developer Discord Accounts
description: A malicious PyPI package in the GitHub supply chain attack campaign accessed Discord application data directories, decrypted locally stored Discord authentication tokens, and exfiltrated them to attacker infrastructure enabling complete takeover of victim Discord accounts.
label: AV-400

title: Supply Chain Malware Steals Cryptocurrency Wallets and Telegram Data from Compromised Developer Systems
description: A malicious package distributed through a compromised GitHub supply chain attack stole cryptocurrency wallet files and Telegram session data from infected developer machines, combining financial theft with communication account compromise.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Supply Chain Package Steals Instagram Session Tokens from Compromised Developer Machines
description: A malicious package distributed through a typosquatted Python mirror registered as files.pypihosted.org stole Instagram session tokens from developer machines, targeting social media authentication credentials alongside financial and communication data.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Rust Crate rustdecimal Typosquats rust_decimal Financial Library on Crates.io
description: The malicious Rust crate rustdecimal typosquatted the popular rust_decimal financial calculation library on crates.io, targeting Rust developers working on financial applications and attempting to exfiltrate sensitive configuration data from systems where it was installed.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Rust Crate ferris-says-rs Typosquats ferris-says on Crates.io Targeting Beginners
description: A malicious Rust crate named ferris-says-rs typosquatted the popular ferris-says learning tool on crates.io, specifically targeting Rust beginners who are likely to install tutorial packages without careful verification of package names.
label: AV-200

title: Rust Cargo Dependency Confusion Attack via Crates.io and Private Registry Name Collision
description: Dependency confusion attacks targeting the Rust Cargo ecosystem exploit name collisions between private internal crates and public crates.io packages, causing Cargo's dependency resolution to automatically prefer public malicious crates over private internal ones when version numbers are higher.
label: AV-509

title: event-stream npm Package Compromise Targets Copay Bitcoin Wallet to Steal Cryptocurrency Funds
description: The event-stream npm package was compromised by a new maintainer who added the flatmap-stream dependency containing obfuscated code that specifically targeted the Copay Bitcoin wallet application to steal funds from wallets with balances exceeding 100 Bitcoin.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Installs Hidden Cryptominer Using Victim CPU Resources for Monero Mining
description: Multiple malicious npm packages documented in security research installed hidden cryptocurrency mining software that silently consumed victim CPU and GPU resources to mine Monero cryptocurrency for attackers while running in the background of developer systems.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Uses DNS Queries to Exfiltrate Stolen Credentials Bypassing HTTP Monitoring
description: A malicious npm package documented in supply chain security research used DNS query encoding to exfiltrate stolen credentials and environment variables, bypassing HTTP and HTTPS network monitoring tools by encoding sensitive data as subdomain labels of attacker-controlled DNS queries.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Encodes AWS Credentials into DNS TXT Queries to Evade Data Loss Prevention
description: A malicious PyPI package used DNS TXT record queries to exfiltrate AWS access keys and secret tokens by encoding them as base64 strings in DNS query hostnames, evading enterprise Data Loss Prevention systems that monitor HTTP traffic but not DNS queries.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Acts as Ransomware Dropper Encrypting Developer Project Files After Installation
description: Multiple malicious npm packages documented in security research acted as ransomware droppers that encrypted developer project files and local code repositories after installation, demanding cryptocurrency ransom payments and causing significant development disruption.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Erases Entire Codebase After Installation Causing Catastrophic Data Loss
description: Several malicious npm packages documented by Checkmarx, ReversingLabs, Safety, and Socket erased entire developer codebases after installation, performing recursive deletion of project directories and causing catastrophic and irreversible data loss to affected developers.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious RubyGems and npm Packages Steal Telegram API Tokens from Developer Environments
description: Multiple malicious RubyGems and npm packages discovered by Checkmarx and Socket drained and exfiltrated Telegram API tokens from developer environments, enabling attackers to access Telegram Bot accounts and message histories of affected developers.
label: AV-400

title: Sonatype Identifies Combosquatting npm Packages Combining Two Legitimate Library Names to Deceive Developers
description: Sonatype researchers identified combosquatting npm packages that combined the names of two popular legitimate libraries such as react-dom-router or webpack-babel-loader, exploiting developer familiarity with both component names to increase installation credibility and bypass name-based detection.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package Uses Soundsquatting to Target Developers Installing Packages by Voice Search or Autocomplete
description: Security researchers documented soundsquatting attacks on PyPI where malicious packages used phonetically similar names to legitimate libraries, specifically targeting developers using voice search or IDE autocomplete features that suggest packages based on pronunciation similarity.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious npm Package Uses Internationalized Domain Name Homograph Attack to Impersonate Trusted Registry
description: A malicious npm package combined IDN homograph attacks with typosquatting by creating package names using Unicode characters that render identically to ASCII in some display contexts, impersonating trusted packages in ways that bypassed text-based security scanning tools.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package Impersonates Popular Security Tool bandit to Target Security-Conscious Developers
description: A malicious PyPI package impersonated the popular bandit Python security linting tool, specifically targeting security-conscious developers who actively install security tools, exploiting the irony that developers installing security tools are particularly attractive targets for credential theft.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Affect Kotlin Android Applications Enabling Hidden Backdoors in Mobile Apps
description: Security researchers confirmed that the Trojan Source vulnerability affects Kotlin source code used for Android development, where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters can alter conditional logic and string handling in mobile applications in ways invisible to code reviewers using standard Android Studio.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in PHP Applications Enable Hidden SQL Injection and Authentication Bypass
description: The PHPCSStandards project documented Trojan Source attacks in PHP where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters enable invisible SQL injection payloads and authentication bypass logic embedded in PHP web application code that appears completely safe during manual and automated code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Vulnerability in TypeScript Compiler Enables Hidden Logic in Typed Code with Full Type Safety Appearance
description: The Trojan Source research confirmed TypeScript is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks where hidden characters can alter the behavior of TypeScript code while maintaining complete type safety appearance, causing TypeScript's static type checker to pass malicious code as safe.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Embedded in GitHub README and Documentation Files Deliver Malicious Instructions
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks using Unicode characters embedded in GitHub README markdown files and documentation, causing rendered documentation to display different installation or configuration instructions from the raw markdown source visible to repository maintainers.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Affects C# .NET Applications Enabling Hidden Logic in Enterprise Software Development
description: The original Trojan Source research confirmed C# and the .NET ecosystem are vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks, with particular concern for enterprise software development where C# applications often handle sensitive financial and healthcare data.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Jupyter Notebook Cells Enable Hidden Data Exfiltration in Data Science Workflows
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Jupyter notebook code cells where hidden Unicode characters alter data processing logic, enabling silent exfiltration of sensitive training datasets and model outputs in data science and machine learning workflows.
label: AV-300

title: Jenkins Groovy Pipeline Script Injection via Unsanitized SCM Checkout Enables Arbitrary Command Execution
description: Jenkins pipeline poisoning attacks exploit unsanitized SCM checkout operations where Jenkinsfile changes from untrusted branches are executed with full pipeline permissions, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary Groovy code that executes with Jenkins agent credentials and workspace access.
label: AV-410

title: GitLab CI Pipeline Poisoning via Include Directive Loading Malicious External Configuration
description: GitLab CI pipeline poisoning attacks exploit the include directive that loads external YAML configuration files, where attackers modify included configuration sources to inject malicious pipeline stages that execute with the target repository's CI/CD tokens and deployment credentials.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised Python pytest Plugin Injects Malicious Test Fixtures Executing During CI Test Runs
description: A compromised Python pytest plugin injected malicious test fixtures that executed credential-stealing code during CI test runs, exploiting the elevated trust that test frameworks receive during build processes to exfiltrate secrets from CI environments.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious VS Code Extension Harvests Authentication Tokens from Developer Workstations via IDE Plugin Supply Chain
description: A malicious VS Code extension distributed through the Visual Studio Code marketplace harvested GitHub authentication tokens, SSH keys, and cloud credentials from developer workstations, demonstrating IDE plugin supply chains as an undermonitored vector for developer credential theft.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised ESLint npm Package Transforms Linting Tool into Credential Theft Vector Across Development Pipelines
description: The compromised eslint-scope npm package demonstrated how widely-used linting and static analysis tools can be transformed into credential theft vectors, affecting all CI pipelines that run linting as part of their build process and gaining access to npm authentication tokens.
label: AV-410

title: Rust Cargo Workspace Dependency Confusion via Local Path Override and Registry Fallback Exploitation
description: Dependency confusion attacks targeting Rust Cargo workspaces exploit the interaction between local path dependencies and registry fallback resolution, where incorrectly configured Cargo.toml workspace members can resolve dependencies from public crates.io instead of intended local paths.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus 2 Enables Stored XSS Leading to Maven Artifact Poisoning
description: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus 2 documented a stored XSS vulnerability in Maven proxy repository management that could be exploited to poison artifact caching, causing malicious artifacts to be served to downstream Java applications fetching dependencies through the compromised Nexus instance.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven Repository Enables Path Traversal for Artifact Poisoning
description: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven repository documented a path traversal vulnerability that allowed attackers to write arbitrary files to the server, enabling poisoning of cached Maven artifacts served to downstream Java builds.
label: AV-509

title: Fake pypihosted.org Python Mirror Typosquats Official pythonhosted.org to Distribute Malicious Packages
description: Attackers registered the typosquatted domain files.pypihosted.org closely resembling the official files.pythonhosted.org Python package mirror, using it to distribute malicious PyPI packages linked to popular GitHub projects and targeting developers who accidentally used the fake mirror.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious npm Package reakt Typosquats react Library Targeting React Framework Developers
description: The malicious npm package reakt used a one-character transposition to typosquat the extremely popular React JavaScript library, targeting the massive React developer ecosystem and executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts on developer machines installing what they believed was the React framework.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Kubernetes YAML Manifests Alter Pod Security Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Kubernetes YAML manifests where hidden Unicode characters alter pod security context configurations, causing containers to run with elevated privileges or disabled security controls invisible to infrastructure reviewers.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in GitHub Actions YAML Alter Conditional Job Execution Logic
description: Trojan Source attacks embedded in GitHub Actions workflow YAML files use hidden Unicode characters to alter conditional job execution logic, causing if conditions on security-critical workflow steps to evaluate differently from their visual representation and bypassing manual workflow review.
label: AV-300

title: Compromised prettier Code Formatter npm Package Transforms Formatting Step into Credential Exfiltration
description: A compromised version of the prettier code formatter npm package weaponized the widely-used formatting step in development workflows, executing credential-stealing code during routine code formatting operations that occur frequently in both local development and CI pipeline runs.
label: AV-410

title: CircleCI Orb Supply Chain Compromise Injects Malicious Steps into Shared Pipeline Configurations
description: A supply chain compromise targeting CircleCI orbs injected malicious pipeline steps into widely-shared reusable CircleCI configurations, affecting all pipelines that imported the compromised orb and gaining access to environment variables and deployment credentials across multiple organizations.
label: AV-410

title: MavenGate Attacker Uses GitHub Actions Token Theft to Publish Malicious Artifacts to Maven Central
description: MavenGate attackers combined GitHub Actions token theft with expired Maven groupId takeover to publish malicious artifacts directly to Maven Central, using stolen CI credentials to authenticate package publishing and bypass manual artifact review processes.
label: AV-509

title: Supply Chain Malware Extracts Browser Autofill Data Including Saved Passwords and Credit Card Numbers
description: A malicious package distributed through a compromised GitHub supply chain systematically extracted browser autofill data from Chrome, Edge, Opera, and Yandex browsers including saved passwords, credit card numbers, and form autofill information stored in browser credential stores.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Packages Typosquat Popular AI and LLM SDK Libraries Targeting AI Developer Ecosystem
description: A growing trend of malicious PyPI packages documented by security researchers typosquat popular AI and LLM SDK libraries including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Hugging Face client packages, targeting the rapidly growing AI developer ecosystem where developers frequently install new packages.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Rust Crate Executes Data Destruction Payload Wiping Cargo Workspace and Git Repository
description: A malicious Rust crate documented in supply chain security research executed a data destruction payload that recursively deleted the victim's Cargo workspace files and Git repository history upon compilation, causing catastrophic and irreversible loss of development work.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Makefiles Alter Build Target Execution Order and Commands
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks in Makefile build configurations where hidden Unicode characters alter build target execution order and shell command interpretation, causing make build systems to execute different commands from what developers see when reviewing build configurations.
label: AV-300

title: Compromised Maven Build Plugin Executes Malicious Lifecycle Phase During Java Application Compilation
description: A compromised Maven build plugin injected malicious execution into the compile lifecycle phase of Java applications, stealing Maven credentials, Git configuration, and cloud provider tokens from developer workstations during routine build operations.
label: AV-410

title: CVE-2025-30066: tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action Compromised via Renovate Bot Impersonation to Expose Secrets in 23,000 Repositories
description: Attackers compromised the tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action (CVE-2025-30066) by spoofing a commit as the legitimate renovate[bot] using a stolen GitHub PAT, injecting a base64-encoded Node.js payload that downloaded a malicious Python script scanning Runner Worker memory for secrets and printing them in workflow logs of over 23,000 affected repositories.
label: AV-410

title: CVE-2025-30154: reviewdog/action-setup GitHub Action Compromised Enabling Chain Attack on tj-actions Organization
description: The reviewdog/action-setup GitHub Action was compromised (CVE-2025-30154) enabling a chained supply chain attack where stolen credentials propagated from reviewdog to the tj-actions organization, demonstrating how a single GitHub Action compromise can cascade through interconnected action ecosystems.
label: AV-410

title: spotbugs/sonar-findbugs pull_request_target Workflow Leaks PAT Enabling tj-actions Compromise Chain
description: A malicious pull request submitted to spotbugs/sonar-findbugs exploited a pull_request_target workflow trigger that used a maintainer PAT as a secret, leaking the token that was subsequently used to invite a malicious actor to the spotbugs organization and initiate the chain of compromises ending with the tj-actions/changed-files supply chain attack.
label: AV-410

title: Coinbase Targeted via tj-actions/changed-files CVE-2025-30066 Compromise Affecting 70,000 Customers
description: Attackers specifically targeted Coinbase through the tj-actions/changed-files supply chain attack, leveraging exposed secrets to access Coinbase CI/CD pipelines in an attack that ultimately affected approximately 70,000 Coinbase customers and highlighted how targeted supply chain attacks can cause widespread collateral damage.
label: AV-410

title: Ultralytics GitHub Actions Cache Poisoning via pull_request_target Enables Malicious PyPI Package Publication
description: The Ultralytics YOLO repository suffered a supply chain attack where shell injection through a pull_request_target trigger was combined with GitHub Actions cache poisoning to gain access to the PyPI publishing pipeline, resulting in the publication of malicious Ultralytics package versions to PyPI.
label: AV-410

title: Kong Ingress Controller GitHub Actions Workflow Bypassed via Dependabot Impersonation Enabling PAT Exfiltration
description: The Kong Ingress Controller's GitHub Actions workflow restricted pull_request_target execution to trusted users but was bypassed through Dependabot impersonation, allowing attackers to exfiltrate a GitHub PAT and use it to compromise downstream dependencies.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud npm Worm Automatically Propagates Malicious Postinstall Scripts via Compromised npm Tokens Across Packages
description: The Shai-Hulud malware demonstrated worm-like behavior by using compromised npm tokens to automatically publish malicious postinstall scripts to multiple npm packages and compromised GitHub tokens to inject malicious GitHub Actions workflows, creating self-propagating supply chain infections across interconnected packages.
label: AV-410

title: npm debug chalk and color Package Maintainer Phished via Fake 2FA Update Email Enabling Account Takeover
description: Maintainers of the widely-used npm packages debug, chalk, and color were targeted by phishing emails impersonating GitHub's 2FA update process, tricking maintainers with TOTP 2FA enabled into surrendering their credentials and enabling attackers to take over the accounts and publish malicious package versions.
label: AV-200

title: polyfill.io Supply Chain Attack via CDN Domain Purchase Delivers Malicious JavaScript to 100,000+ Websites
description: Attackers purchased the polyfill.io CDN domain and GitHub organization after a control handoff, redirecting the widely-used JavaScript polyfill service to deliver malicious scripts to over 100,000 websites that had embedded the CDN link, demonstrating the risk of third-party CDN dependency in web supply chains.
label: AV-509

title: Lazarus Group Operation 99 Targets Web3 and Cryptocurrency Developers via Fake Job Repository Supply Chain Attack
description: The Lazarus Group's Operation 99 campaign targeted Web3 and cryptocurrency developers by creating fake job offer repositories that contained malicious code executed during CI/CD evaluation, exfiltrating developer credentials and private keys from compromised development environments.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Malware Downloads TruffleHog Secret Scanner to Systematically Find and Exfiltrate All Developer Secrets
description: The Shai-Hulud supply chain malware downloaded the legitimate TruffleHog secret scanning tool after installation, using it to systematically scan developer file systems and git histories for all committed secrets, API keys, and credentials before exfiltrating the comprehensive results to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: Koi Security Discovers Malicious VS Code Extensions on OpenVSX Marketplace Harvesting Developer Secrets
description: Koi Security discovered malicious VS Code extensions distributed through the OpenVSX marketplace that harvested developer secrets, SSH keys, and authentication tokens from developer workstations, demonstrating the expanding attack surface of IDE extension marketplaces as supply chain vectors.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Actions Mutable Version Tags Enable Instant Compromise of All Dependent Workflows Upon Tag Override
description: Security researchers documented a fundamental design vulnerability in GitHub Actions where mutable version tags allow attackers who compromise an action repository to instantly compromise every workflow depending on that action by overriding existing tags to point to malicious commits, unlike immutable package systems used by Go and other ecosystems.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised GitHub Actions Workflows Bypass Trusted Publishing by Stealing OIDC Tokens During Legitimate Workflow Execution
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where compromised GitHub Actions workflows steal OpenID Connect tokens during legitimate workflow execution, bypassing trusted publishing security controls by obtaining OIDC tokens generated for authorized publishing steps and using them to authenticate malicious package uploads.
label: AV-410

title: GitHub npm Scanning Detects Hundreds of Malicious Package Versions Daily Confirming Industrial Scale of Supply Chain Attacks
description: GitHub's npm malware scanning program confirmed that hundreds of newly published npm package versions contain malicious code daily, with human review confirming true positives before action is taken, demonstrating that typosquatting and package compromise have reached industrial scale in the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-200

title: MavenGate Vulnerability Affects Dependencies of Google Facebook Signal and Amazon Applications via Expired GroupIds
description: The MavenGate attack demonstrated that dependencies used by applications from Google, Facebook, Signal, and Amazon were vulnerable to groupId hijacking via expired domain purchases, with researchers finding 6,170 of 33,938 analyzed Maven groupId domains available for purchase enabling silent injection of malicious code.
label: AV-509

title: MavenGate Enables Supply Chain Attacks Targeting Google Play Store Android Applications via Abandoned Maven GroupIds
description: The MavenGate supply chain attack method specifically threatened Android applications distributed through the Google Play Store, where expired Maven groupIds used by popular Android libraries could be purchased by attackers to inject malicious code into apps affecting millions of end users.
label: AV-509

title: Cl0p Ransomware Gang Exploits Oracle E-Business Suite Zero-Day CVE-2025-61882 in Supply Chain Extortion Campaign
description: The Cl0p ransomware gang exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite (CVE-2025-61882) in a supply chain extortion campaign discovered by Google and Mandiant, stealing private files from victims including NHS England, Schneider Electric, Harvard University, American Airlines, and The Washington Post.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in AI Model Training Scripts Enable Hidden Data Poisoning of Machine Learning Models
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in AI model training scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter data preprocessing and label assignment logic, enabling silent data poisoning that causes trained models to exhibit hidden malicious behaviors invisible during training code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Solidity Smart Contracts Enable Hidden Token Theft Logic Invisible in Code Review
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Solidity smart contracts where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters alter conditional logic and token transfer permissions, enabling hidden fund theft mechanisms that appear as legitimate token management code during smart contract security audits.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Hugging Face Model Repository Typosquats Popular AI Models to Distribute Pickle-Based Malware
description: Malicious Hugging Face model repositories used typosquatted names mimicking popular AI models including GPT and BERT variants, distributing malicious model files containing pickle-based payloads that executed arbitrary code when researchers and developers loaded the models for evaluation.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Pickle Files in PyPI AI Packages Execute Arbitrary Code When Machine Learning Models Are Loaded
description: Multiple malicious PyPI packages targeting the machine learning ecosystem distributed malicious pickle serialization files disguised as pre-trained model weights, executing arbitrary credential-stealing code when data scientists and AI researchers loaded the files using standard PyTorch and scikit-learn model loading functions.
label: AV-400

title: MavenGate Username Resurrection Attack Reclaims Abandoned GitHub Usernames to Regain Control of Connected Maven Packages
description: The MavenGate attack method included a username resurrection variant where attackers reclaimed abandoned GitHub usernames previously associated with Maven package maintainers, automatically regaining control of Maven packages linked to those GitHub accounts through Apache Maven's GitHub-based authentication.
label: AV-509

title: Sandworm UAC-0212 Targets ICS Supply Chain via CVE-2024-38213 to Steal Engineering Schematics from Critical Infrastructure
description: The Sandworm subcluster UAC-0212 conducted supply chain attacks against critical infrastructure in Ukraine, Serbia, and Czech Republic by exploiting CVE-2024-38213 via phishing LNK files to gain initial access, then harvesting ICS credentials and engineering schematics for further supply chain compromise of industrial control systems.
label: AV-410

title: Phishing Attack Bypasses npm Trusted Publishing by Stealing TOTP Codes from Package Maintainers
description: Security researchers documented phishing attacks specifically designed to bypass npm trusted publishing protections by intercepting TOTP authentication codes from package maintainers in real-time, enabling attackers to take over publishing sessions and release malicious package versions before TOTP codes expire.
label: AV-200

title: SimpleHelp Vulnerabilities CVE-2024-57726 CVE-2024-57727 CVE-2024-57728 Exploited to Deploy DragonForce Ransomware via MSP Supply Chain
description: Sophos researchers documented exploitation of chained SimpleHelp vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-57726, CVE-2024-57727, CVE-2024-57728) to target a managed service provider and deploy DragonForce ransomware across multiple client endpoints, demonstrating how MSP supply chain compromise amplifies ransomware impact.
label: AV-400

title: CVE-2024-5083 Sonatype Nexus 2 Stored XSS Enables Maven Artifact Cache Poisoning for Downstream Build Compromise
description: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus Repository 2 documented a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Maven proxy management that attackers could chain with cache poisoning, causing Nexus to serve malicious Maven artifacts to all downstream Java builds consuming dependencies through the compromised repository.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-36115 Reposilite Path Traversal Enables Writing Arbitrary Maven Artifacts to Poison Java Dependency Builds
description: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven repository manager documented a path traversal vulnerability allowing attackers to write arbitrary files to the server file system, enabling poisoning of Maven artifacts served to downstream Java application builds consuming dependencies through the compromised Reposilite instance.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Rust Crate cargo-auditt Typosquats Security Tool cargo-audit Targeting Security-Focused Rust Developers
description: A malicious Rust crate named cargo-auditt used a double-t typosquatting technique to impersonate the legitimate cargo-audit security vulnerability scanner, specifically targeting security-conscious Rust developers who actively audit their dependencies and are likely to install security tooling without careful name verification.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Rust Procedural Macros Enable Hidden Code Injection at Compile Time
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks specifically targeting Rust procedural macros where hidden Unicode characters alter macro expansion behavior at compile time, enabling injection of hidden malicious code into all crates that use the compromised macro without any visible indication in the macro source.
label: AV-300

title: XZ Utils Backdoor CVE-2024-3094: Nation-State Actor Embeds SSH Authentication Bypass in Widely-Used Compression Library
description: A nation-state actor using the persona Jia Tan spent years building trust as an XZ Utils maintainer before embedding a sophisticated backdoor (CVE-2024-3094) that bypassed SSH authentication in systems using the compromised liblzma library, affecting multiple major Linux distributions in one of the most sophisticated supply chain attacks ever discovered.
label: AV-400

title: XZ Utils CVE-2024-3094: Two-Year Social Engineering Campaign Gains Maintainer Trust Before Backdoor Insertion
description: The XZ Utils backdoor attack (CVE-2024-3094) demonstrated an unprecedented two-year social engineering campaign where the attacker Jia Tan gradually built credibility as a legitimate contributor before gaining maintainer access and inserting a binary backdoor hidden in test files, representing a new category of long-horizon supply chain attack.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Linux Kernel Patch Submissions Enable Hidden Logic in Security-Critical Code
description: Security researchers demonstrated that Trojan Source attacks could be embedded in Linux kernel patch submissions where hidden Unicode characters alter memory allocation and access control logic, passing automated patch analysis while executing fundamentally different code than what kernel reviewers see during the review process.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious PyPI Package langchainn Typosquats langchain AI Framework to Target AI Application Developers
description: A malicious PyPI package named langchainn used a character doubling technique to typosquat the extremely popular langchain AI application framework, targeting the rapidly growing AI developer community building LLM-powered applications and executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts.
label: AV-200

title: npm Trusted Publishing Gap Allows Malicious Package Publication Without Provenance When Registry Accepts Unsigned Packages
description: Security researchers documented a trusted publishing gap in npm where the registry continued to accept and serve unsigned package versions even after trusted publishing was enabled, allowing attackers with stolen npm tokens to publish malicious packages that lacked provenance attestation alongside legitimate signed versions.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Actions Workflow Injection via Unsanitized Issue Title Bypasses CodeQL Security Analysis
description: Security researchers documented GitHub Actions workflow injection attacks where unsanitized issue titles were interpolated into shell commands in workflow files, enabling arbitrary command execution and secret exfiltration in a pattern that bypassed CodeQL analysis because the vulnerability appeared in workflow configuration rather than application code.
label: AV-410

title: SolarWinds Orion Build System Compromised to Distribute SUNBURST Backdoor to 18,000 Government and Enterprise Customers
description: Nation-state attackers compromised the SolarWinds Orion build system to inject the SUNBURST backdoor into official signed software updates, distributing it to approximately 18,000 customers including US government agencies and Fortune 500 enterprises in one of the most impactful supply chain attacks in history.
label: AV-400

title: left-pad npm Package Removal Breaks Thousands of Projects Demonstrating Critical Dependency Risk in npm Ecosystem
description: The removal of the left-pad npm package by its author broke thousands of dependent projects worldwide including Babel and React build tools, demonstrating how the npm ecosystem's dependence on small transitive packages creates catastrophic supply chain fragility even without malicious intent.
label: AV-200

title: Docker Hub Namespace Squatting Attack Registers Official-Looking Image Names Before Legitimate Projects Can Claim Them
description: Security researchers documented Docker Hub namespace squatting attacks where attackers registered image names in the official Docker Hub namespace that closely matched popular open source projects before the projects could claim their official namespace, causing users to inadvertently pull attacker-controlled container images.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Actions Cache Poisoning Attack Injects Malicious Build Artifacts via Shared Cache Between Pull Requests and Main Workflows
description: Security researchers documented GitHub Actions cache poisoning attacks where pull requests from forked repositories poison shared build caches that are subsequently consumed by privileged main branch workflows, enabling attackers to inject malicious compiled artifacts into official software releases.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Ansible Playbooks Alter Task Execution Order and Host Targeting Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Ansible playbook YAML files where hidden Unicode characters alter task execution order, when conditions, and host targeting logic, causing infrastructure automation to configure systems differently from what DevOps engineers see during playbook review.
label: AV-300

title: PyPI Package Name Collision with GitHub Repository Names Causes pip Install Confusion Attacks
description: Security researchers documented pip install confusion attacks where PyPI package names collide with GitHub repository names commonly used in pip install commands with VCS URLs, causing developers who mistype or copy-paste install commands to install malicious PyPI packages instead of the intended GitHub repository sources.
label: AV-200

title: 3CX Desktop Application Supply Chain Attack Delivers Information Stealer via Compromised Build Process
description: The 3CX Desktop Application suffered a supply chain attack where the Windows and macOS desktop clients were trojanized through a compromised build process, delivering an information stealer payload to thousands of enterprise customers using the legitimate signed application distributed through official channels.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Mirror Intercepts pip Downloads via DNS Hijacking to Serve Trojanized Package Versions
description: Security researchers documented attacks where malicious PyPI mirrors deployed through DNS hijacking intercepted pip download requests and served trojanized versions of legitimate packages with embedded malware, affecting developers in networks where DNS had been compromised or whose pip configuration pointed to the malicious mirror.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Bidirectional Characters Affect Ruby Interpreter Enabling Hidden Logic in Rails Applications
description: The Trojan Source research confirmed the Ruby interpreter is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks, with particular impact on Ruby on Rails web applications where hidden characters can alter authentication logic, ActiveRecord query conditions, and controller action routing invisible during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Long-Lived npm Publish Tokens Enable Persistent Unauthorized Package Publication After Initial Credential Compromise
description: Security researchers and GitHub documented the critical role of long-lived npm publish tokens in supply chain attacks, where a single token compromise enables persistent unauthorized package publication across all packages the maintainer controls, with token theft often going undetected for months before malicious package versions are discovered.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious PyPI Packages Exploit Rapid AI SDK Release Cycles to Publish Typosquatted Versions Before Official Releases
description: Security researchers documented malicious PyPI packages exploiting the rapid release cycles of AI SDK libraries including those for OpenAI and Anthropic models, publishing typosquatted package names in the window between official version announcements and actual PyPI publication to maximize installation rates among eager developers.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised GitHub Actions Workflows Abuse OIDC Federation to Access AWS Azure and GCP Cloud Infrastructure
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where compromised GitHub Actions workflows abuse OIDC federation trust relationships between GitHub and cloud providers, using legitimate workflow OIDC tokens to access AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud production infrastructure without needing long-lived cloud credentials.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Nginx and Apache Configuration Files Alter Routing and Access Control Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Nginx and Apache web server configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter URL routing rules, access control directives, and proxy pass configurations, causing web servers to route traffic differently from what administrators see in configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Jupyter Notebook Shared via GitHub Executes Hidden Credential Theft When Data Scientists Open and Run Cells
description: Malicious Jupyter notebooks shared through GitHub repositories and data science communities contained hidden credential-stealing code embedded in markdown cells rendered as documentation, executing credential theft when data scientists opened and ran the notebooks believing them to be legitimate tutorials or datasets.
label: AV-400

title: CISA Alert: Shai-Hulud npm Worm Compromises 500+ Packages Exfiltrating AWS GCP and Azure Credentials
description: CISA issued an alert on the Shai-Hulud npm worm that compromised over 500 npm packages by gaining initial access via phishing, scanning environments for GitHub PATs and cloud API keys for AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure, exfiltrating credentials to attacker-controlled endpoints, and using GitHub REST API to spread automatically to additional maintainer accounts.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Exfiltrates All CI/CD Secrets via HTTP Requests to Attacker-Controlled Webhook Endpoints
description: The Shai-Hulud worm compromised GitHub Actions workflows to enumerate all secrets exposed during CI/CD runtime, packaged them into transmission payloads, and sent them via HTTP requests to attacker-controlled webhook endpoints including webhook.site domains, affecting organizations across North America and Europe.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Uses GitHub REST API to Identify and Compromise All Repositories Accessible to Victim Account
description: The Shai-Hulud worm automated lateral movement by querying the GitHub REST API endpoint /user/repos with affiliation=owner,collaborator,organization_member to enumerate all repositories accessible to compromised developer accounts, then injecting malicious workflows into each identified repository to maximize attack spread.
label: AV-410

title: Attacker Breaches Qix npm Maintainer via Fraudulent support@npmjs.help 2FA Reset Email Compromising 18 Packages
description: Attackers breached the high-profile npm maintainer Qix through a sophisticated phishing campaign using the fraudulent domain support@npmjs.help to imitate a 2FA authentication reset email, gaining access to inject obfuscated browser-based cryptocurrency-stealing malware into 18 popular npm packages including debug, chalk, and ansi-styles.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised debug chalk and ansi-styles npm Packages Inject Crypto Stealer Downloaded 2.5 Million Times
description: Following the phishing compromise of maintainer Qix, malicious versions of debug, chalk, and ansi-styles npm packages containing obfuscated browser-based cryptocurrency-stealing malware hooking browser APIs to hijack transactions were downloaded over 2.5 million times before removal.
label: AV-400

title: GhostActions Campaign Compromises PyPI Maintainer Account to Insert Malicious GitHub Actions Workflow Exfiltrating 3000+ Secrets
description: The GhostActions campaign leveraged a compromised PyPI maintainer account on September 2 2025 to insert a malicious GitHub Actions workflow into package repositories, systematically stealing secrets from Actions environments and confirmed to have exfiltrated over 3,000 secrets including npm tokens used in subsequent supply chain attack waves.
label: AV-410

title: GHSA-7v68-3pr5-h3cr: @lottiefiles/lottie-player Compromised via Stolen npm Token Deploys Web3 Wallet-Draining Script
description: The @lottiefiles/lottie-player npm package versions 2.0.5, 2.0.6, and 2.0.7 were compromised via a stolen npm token injecting a wallet-draining script targeting Web3 cryptocurrency sites, discovered within hours when a developer at a crypto firm noticed an unusual transaction traced back to a Lottie animation.
label: AV-400

title: CVE-2024-54134: @solana/web3.js Versions 1.95.6 and 1.95.7 Backdoored to Exfiltrate Private Keys to Hardcoded Wallet
description: CVE-2024-54134 documents that @solana/web3.js versions 1.95.6 and 1.95.7 shipped with a backdoor that exfiltrated Solana wallet private keys to a hardcoded attacker-controlled wallet address, affecting Solana DeFi applications and developers building cryptocurrency applications on the Solana blockchain ecosystem.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Packages termncolor and colorinal Downloaded 884 Times Deploy DLL Side-Loading Malware with C2 Communication
description: The malicious PyPI packages termncolor and colorinal were downloaded 884 times before discovery, exploiting DLL side-loading vulnerabilities to execute malicious code, establishing persistence mechanisms, and communicating with a command-and-control infrastructure via the Zulip messaging platform where the threat actor had been active since July 2025.
label: AV-200

title: SlowMist Documents Fake Job Assessment npm Repository Harvesting iCloud Keychain Browser Data and Cryptocurrency Wallets
description: SlowMist researchers documented a social engineering supply chain attack where threat actors tricked developers into cloning a GitHub repository disguised as a job technical assessment containing a booby-trapped npm package that harvested iCloud Keychain data, web browser credentials, and cryptocurrency wallet information before exfiltrating to an external server.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Job Assessment npm Package Downloads Python Scripts to Log Keystrokes Take Screenshots and Steal Credentials
description: A malicious npm package disguised as part of a developer job technical assessment downloaded Python scripts that logged all keystrokes, took periodic screenshots, scanned file systems for sensitive files, stole browser and application credentials, and captured clipboard contents before transmitting the comprehensive data collection to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Implements Expired Domain Check to Prevent Domain Resurrection Supply Chain Account Takeover Attacks
description: PyPI implemented a new security feature checking for expired domains associated with maintainer accounts after documenting domain resurrection attacks where attackers purchased expired domains linked to PyPI maintainer email addresses to trigger password reset flows and take over package publishing accounts.
label: AV-509

title: PyPI Security Responds to GitHub Actions Campaign Stealing PyPI Publishing Tokens from Workflow Secrets
description: PyPI security administrators responded to an attack campaign where malicious actors injected code into GitHub Actions workflows targeting repositories with PyPI tokens stored as GitHub secrets, modifying workflows to transmit tokens to external servers before invaliding all affected tokens and notifying impacted maintainers.
label: AV-410

title: Socket Documents npm Phishing Email Campaign Using Typosquatted Domain to Target Package Maintainers
description: Socket researchers documented an npm phishing email campaign using a typosquatted domain closely resembling the official npm support address, sending fraudulent security notifications to package maintainers to steal credentials and gain unauthorized publishing access to widely-used npm packages.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Targets CrowdStrike npm Packages in Updated Supply Chain Attack Campaign
description: An updated wave of the Shai-Hulud npm supply chain attack specifically targeted CrowdStrike npm packages, with Socket researchers identifying ongoing compromise attempts against the cybersecurity company's npm ecosystem presence in a campaign that demonstrated the attackers' sophistication in targeting high-value security vendor packages.
label: AV-410

title: pnpm and yarn Introduce Minimum Package Age Enforcement to Prevent Flash Malicious Publish Supply Chain Attacks
description: Following the Shai-Hulud and related npm supply chain attacks, pnpm and yarn JavaScript package managers introduced optional minimum package age enforcement settings that quarantine newly published package versions for a configurable cooling period before allowing installation, catching most flash-malicious-publish events before they reach production.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Cryptohijacker Payload Hooks Browser APIs to Silently Replace Cryptocurrency Wallet Addresses in Transactions
description: The Cryptohijacker payload delivered by the Shai-Hulud npm worm hooked browser JavaScript APIs to intercept cryptocurrency transactions, silently replacing legitimate recipient wallet addresses with attacker-controlled addresses at the moment of transaction confirmation without any visible indication to the victim.
label: AV-400

title: S1ngularity Attack Steals npm Token by Modifying publish.yml via GITHUB_TOKEN Obtained Through PR Title Injection
description: The S1ngularity attack against Nx used a GITHUB_TOKEN stolen via pull_request_target PR title injection to modify the separate publish.yml workflow, then manually triggered the modified pipeline which sent the NPM_TOKEN secret to an external webhook, enabling direct malicious package publication bypassing GitHub entirely.
label: AV-410

title: cappership Threat Actor Publishes 11 Malicious PyPI Packages Stealing Python Source Code and Solana Private Keys
description: The threat actor using the alias cappership published a second batch of 11 malicious Python packages to PyPI between May 4 and 24 2025, designed to steal Python script files from developer file systems and steal Solana private keys via monkey-patching, transmitting the source code and cryptographic keys to external attacker-controlled servers.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud Second Wave Patches Version Numbers of Infected Packages to Appear as Legitimate Maintainer Updates
description: The second wave of the Shai-Hulud supply chain attack patched the version numbers of infected npm packages to make compromised versions appear as normal routine maintenance updates, specifically designed to blend in with legitimate maintainer activity and reduce the likelihood of security team review before installation.
label: AV-509

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Downloads TruffleHog to Scan Victim Systems for All Secrets Before Self-Replicating to New Packages
description: The Shai-Hulud worm deployed TruffleHog secret scanning tooling to comprehensively enumerate all secrets in compromised developer environments, then used discovered npm and PyPI publish tokens to inject itself into additional packages and publish compromised versions, achieving worm-like self-replication across the JavaScript and Python ecosystems.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in CI Configuration Files Alter Rendered Display in Web Interfaces Versus Actual Execution
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks specifically targeting CI configuration file rendering where hidden Unicode characters cause web-based pipeline visualization interfaces to display different pipeline stages and conditions from what the CI execution engine actually processes, enabling hidden malicious steps invisible in the UI.
label: AV-300

title: Flash Malicious Publish Attacks Exploit Short Window Between npm Package Publication and Security Scanner Detection
description: Security researchers documented flash malicious publish attacks that exploit the window between when malicious npm packages are published and when security scanners detect and flag them, with attackers designed to maximize downloads within this detection gap through coordinated promotion and dependency injection in CI systems that auto-update.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud Malware Uses Organization Scope Detection to Activate Only When Running in Enterprise CI Environments
description: The Shai-Hulud malware implemented conditional activation logic that checked for organizational scope indicators before executing its credential-stealing payload, specifically targeting enterprise CI/CD environments while remaining dormant on individual developer workstations to maximize the value of stolen credentials and reduce detection probability.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Hidden in Python Docstrings Enable Runtime Code Execution via eval Injection
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters embedded in Python docstrings alter how documentation is parsed by doctest and documentation generation tools, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution when documentation strings containing Unicode-obfuscated eval injections are processed.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Cryptohijacker Payload Detected Across Organizations in North America and Europe via Trend Micro Telemetry
description: Trend Micro telemetry confirmed detections of the Shai-Hulud Cryptohijacker payload across organizations in North America and Europe, with the malware hooking browser APIs to redirect cryptocurrency transactions, demonstrating the geographic reach of coordinated npm supply chain attacks on enterprise environments.
label: AV-410

title: pgserve npm Worm Jumps Ecosystems by Using Discovered PyPI Tokens to Inject Malicious Code into Python Packages
description: The malicious pgserve npm package demonstrated cross-ecosystem worm capability by searching compromised developer environments for PyPI publish tokens in addition to npm tokens, using discovered PyPI credentials to inject itself into Python packages and publish malicious versions to PyPI, bridging the JavaScript and Python supply chain ecosystems.
label: AV-200

title: Supply Chain Attacks Succeed Despite SLSA Adoption Due to Gap Between Artifact Provenance and Unsigned Package Acceptance
description: Security researchers documented that SLSA artifact provenance adoption failed to prevent multiple supply chain attacks due to a fundamental gap where package registries continued accepting unsigned packages alongside signed ones, allowing attackers with stolen tokens to publish malicious unsigned packages that appeared alongside legitimate signed versions.
label: AV-509

title: Datadog GuardDog and Supply-Chain Firewall Tools Detect Malicious Packages via Behavioral Heuristics Before Installation
description: Datadog released two open source supply chain security tools responding to the Shai-Hulud and related attacks: GuardDog analyzes metadata and heuristics to flag malicious npm, PyPI, Go, and GitHub Actions packages, while Supply-Chain Firewall prevents installation of malicious packages at the developer workstation level before they reach production.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in OpenAPI Specifications Alter API Security Definitions Visible to Developers
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in OpenAPI specification files where hidden Unicode characters alter security scheme definitions, authentication requirements, and endpoint access controls in ways invisible during API documentation review, causing generated API clients and servers to implement different security policies from what developers see.
label: AV-300

title: Supply Chain Attacks Bypass Sigstore Cosign Keyless Signing When Verification is Not Enforced at Deployment
description: Security researchers documented that Sigstore cosign keyless signing protections were bypassed in multiple supply chain attacks because organizations implemented package signing without enforcing signature verification at deployment time, allowing attackers with stolen tokens to publish unsigned malicious packages that were installed without verification.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious PyPI Packages Typosquat Safety and Bandit Security Scanning Tools to Compromise Security Review Workflows
description: Malicious PyPI packages typosquatted the Safety vulnerability scanner and Bandit security linting tools, targeting developer security review workflows by replacing legitimate security scanning tools with malicious versions that reported false clean results while exfiltrating credentials from systems where security scanning was performed.
label: AV-200

title: MavenGate Exploits Maven Central Failure to Check Domain Ownership Expiration for Existing GroupId Registrations
description: The MavenGate attack exploited Maven Central's failure to periodically verify that domain ownership underlying existing groupId registrations remained valid, allowing attackers who purchased expired domains to immediately claim control of all Maven packages registered under those groupIds without any re-verification process.
label: AV-509

title: Supply Chain Attack on Industrial SCADA Software Update Mechanism Delivers Malware to Critical Infrastructure Operators
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting industrial SCADA software update mechanisms where attackers compromised the software distribution infrastructure of industrial control system vendors, delivering malware alongside legitimate updates to critical infrastructure operators including power plants and water treatment facilities.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Helm Charts Alter Kubernetes Deployment Security Contexts and Resource Limits
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Kubernetes Helm chart templates where hidden Unicode characters alter security context definitions, container resource limits, and network policy configurations in ways invisible during chart review, causing production deployments to apply different security settings than intended.
label: AV-300

title: Wiz Research Documents Widespread GitHub Actions Supply Chain Attacks Increased Significantly in 2025
description: Wiz Research documented a significant increase in GitHub Actions supply chain attacks throughout 2025, with the tj-actions CVE-2025-30066 compromise representing only the most visible incident in a broader trend of attackers systematically targeting GitHub Actions workflows to access CI/CD secrets across thousands of organizations.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Specifically Targets Trusted npm Namespaces to Maximize Developer Trust and Install Rates
description: The Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign deliberately targeted popular and trusted npm package namespaces rather than unknown packages, exploiting the implicit trust developers place in widely-used packages to maximize installation rates of compromised versions and ensure malicious code reached production environments.
label: AV-509

title: PyPI Implements Email Verification for TOTP Logins from New Devices to Counter Phishing-Based Account Takeover
description: PyPI implemented email verification for TOTP-based logins from new or unrecognized devices as a security measure against phishing attacks that steal authentication codes in real-time, adding a second verification layer that prevents attackers from using intercepted TOTP codes to log in from attacker-controlled devices.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Protocol Buffer Definitions Alter gRPC API Field Types and Validation Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Protocol Buffer .proto files where hidden Unicode characters alter field type definitions and validation rules in gRPC API specifications, causing generated client and server code to handle data types and validation differently from what API designers see in the specification review.
label: AV-300

title: TeamPCP Threat Group Attributed to xinference PyPI Payload Targeting AI Infrastructure Developers
description: The TeamPCP threat group was attributed by GitGuardian to the malicious payload injected into three consecutive xinference PyPI package releases, with the credential-stealing second-stage collector specifically targeting AI infrastructure developers using the xinference model serving framework for large language model deployment.
label: AV-400

title: Wiz Research Documents Widespread npm Supply Chain Compromise Impact Across debug chalk and ansi-styles Ecosystem
description: Wiz Research documented the widespread impact of the npm supply chain compromise affecting debug, chalk, and ansi-styles packages through the Qix maintainer phishing attack, analyzing the cascading effects across the JavaScript ecosystem where these foundational packages are used as transitive dependencies by millions of projects.
label: AV-410

title: Second Batch of 11 Solana-Targeting PyPI Packages Steals Python Source Files from Developer File Systems
description: A second batch of 11 malicious PyPI packages targeting the Solana blockchain ecosystem were identified between May 4-24 2025, designed to steal Python source code files from developer file systems and transmit them to external servers, targeting intellectual property alongside cryptographic credentials from Solana developers.
label: AV-200

title: SCA Tools Miss Zero-Day Malicious Packages Because CVE Database Has No Entry on Day Zero of Supply Chain Attack
description: Security researchers documented a fundamental gap in Software Composition Analysis tools where zero-day malicious packages have no CVE entry at the time of attack, causing SCA scanners that match against NVD and ecosystem advisories to completely miss supply chain compromises until CVE assignment hours or weeks after initial deployment.
label: AV-509

title: CanisterSprawl Campaign Uses Internet Computer Protocol Canister C2 Making Infrastructure Takedowns Ineffective
description: The CanisterSprawl supply chain campaign tracked by Socket and StepSecurity used an Internet Computer Protocol canister as command-and-control infrastructure, making traditional takedown approaches ineffective because ICP canisters operate on a decentralized blockchain network that cannot be shut down by conventional law enforcement or hosting provider actions.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in GraphQL Schema Files Alter Query Permission Definitions and Type Resolvers
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in GraphQL schema definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter permission directives, query depth limits, and type resolver assignments in ways invisible during schema review, causing GraphQL API servers to apply different authorization rules from what security architects intended.
label: AV-300

title: Checkmarx KICS VS Code Extension Compromised to Harvest GitHub Tokens AWS and Cloud Credentials from Developer IDEs
description: The official Checkmarx KICS VS Code extension was compromised with an obfuscated payload that harvested GitHub authentication tokens, AWS credentials, and Google Cloud tokens directly from developer IDE environments, demonstrating that security vendor tooling is an increasingly targeted supply chain vector due to elevated trust and broad deployment.
label: AV-400

title: Compromised Namastex.ai npm Packages Combine Credential Theft with Self-Propagation Logic via Stolen Publish Tokens
description: Compromised Namastex.ai npm packages linked to the CanisterSprawl campaign combined install-time credential theft with self-propagation logic, using stolen npm publish tokens to inject malicious code into additional packages the victim could publish, systematically expanding the attack's reach through legitimate publishing infrastructure.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud and S1ngularity Naming Conventions in Repository Descriptions Enable Rapid Incident Investigation and Victim Identification
description: Both the Shai-Hulud and S1ngularity supply chain attacks used distinctive naming conventions in compromised repository descriptions including Shai-Hulud Migration and -migration suffixes and s1ngularity-repository, enabling security researchers to rapidly query GitHub for all affected repositories and notify victims through targeted GitHub search queries.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Environment Variable Names Alter Shell Variable Interpolation in Build Scripts
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks using Unicode bidirectional characters embedded in environment variable names referenced in build scripts, causing shell interpreters to resolve different environment variables from what administrators see in script review, enabling secret substitution and configuration override attacks.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Multi-Wave Campaign Evolves from Opportunistic to Engineered Targeted Attacks Adapting to Defenses
description: GitHub Security documented that the Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign evolved across multiple waves from opportunistic broad compromises to engineered targeted attacks specifically adapting techniques in response to defensive measures implemented after earlier waves, demonstrating that modern supply chain attackers learn rapidly and adjust to ecosystem-level security improvements.
label: AV-509

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Shifts to pre-install Execution Affecting 25,000 GitHub Repositories Across 350 Users
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign reported in November 2025 dramatically widened impact by shifting from postinstall to pre-install hook execution, affecting developer machines before npm install completes, while expanding to over 25,000 malicious repositories across approximately 350 unique GitHub users in the most widespread npm supply chain attack recorded.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Introduces Punitive Sabotage Fallback Destroying Developer Environments When Credential Theft Fails
description: Shai-Hulud 2.0 introduced a far more aggressive fallback mechanism that shifted from pure data theft to punitive sabotage when credential exfiltration failed, deliberately destroying developer environments and corrupting project files to maximize damage even when the primary attack objective was not achieved.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Weaponizes AI-Generated Content for Reconnaissance Accelerating Secret Discovery in Compromised Environments
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign integrated AI-generated content and weaponized AI command-line tools for automated reconnaissance in compromised developer environments, using AI to intelligently identify and prioritize the most valuable secrets and credentials before exfiltration, dramatically accelerating the attack's effectiveness.
label: AV-410

title: GlueStack npm Packages Compromised via lib/commonjs/index.js Injection Affecting Nearly 1 Million Weekly Downloads
description: Aikido Security detected on June 6, 2025 that over a dozen GlueStack npm packages were compromised by injecting malicious code into lib/commonjs/index.js, enabling attackers to run arbitrary commands, capture screenshots, and steal data from the nearly one million weekly download install base of the popular React Native UI component library.
label: AV-400

title: Misconfigured Verdaccio Private npm Proxy Allows Public Registry Fallback Enabling Dependency Confusion Attacks
description: Security researchers documented that incorrectly configured Verdaccio private npm proxy instances with public registry fallback enabled allow dependency confusion attacks where packages not found in the private registry automatically resolve from public npm, enabling attackers to intercept internal package names with malicious public versions.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious npm Package react-native-camera-kit Typosquats Mobile Camera SDK Targeting React Native Developers
description: A malicious npm package typosquatting the popular react-native-camera-kit mobile camera SDK targeted React Native developers building mobile applications, executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts that harvested development environment secrets from compromised developer workstations building iOS and Android applications.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious CocoaPods Package Typosquats Popular iOS Networking Library Targeting Swift and Objective-C Developers
description: Security researchers documented malicious CocoaPods packages typosquatting popular iOS networking libraries such as Alamofire variants, targeting iOS and macOS application developers by injecting credential-stealing code into mobile application build processes through the CocoaPods dependency management system.
label: AV-200

title: CocoaPods Dependency Confusion via CDN Cache Poisoning Enables Malicious Pod Delivery to iOS Application Builds
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against the CocoaPods CDN infrastructure where cache poisoning techniques caused the CocoaPods CDN to serve malicious pod versions to iOS and macOS developers, injecting attacker-controlled code into Apple platform application build processes.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious GitHub App Abuses OAuth Token Scope to Access Private Repositories and Inject Malicious Workflow Files
description: Security researchers documented malicious GitHub Apps that requested broad OAuth token scopes during installation authorization, using the granted permissions to access private repositories, read workflow secrets, and inject malicious GitHub Actions workflow files that exfiltrated CI/CD credentials during pipeline execution.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised GitHub App OAuth Token Used to Publish Malicious Package Versions Bypassing Maintainer 2FA
description: A compromised GitHub App OAuth token was used in a supply chain attack to publish malicious npm and PyPI package versions, bypassing maintainer two-factor authentication requirements because OAuth token-based publishing did not enforce the same 2FA checks as password-based authentication at the time of the attack.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Android SDK Distributed via Compromised Third-Party Repository Injects Adware into Google Play Applications
description: Security researchers documented a malicious Android SDK distributed through a compromised third-party repository that injected adware and data collection code into Android applications built with the SDK, with affected applications passing Google Play Store review and reaching millions of end users through the official app store.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package expo-camera-sdk Typosquats Expo Mobile Framework Targeting Cross-Platform App Developers
description: A malicious npm package named expo-camera-sdk typosquatted the legitimate Expo mobile development framework SDK, targeting React Native developers using Expo to build cross-platform iOS and Android applications and executing credential-stealing code during the expo build process.
label: AV-200

title: Gradle Plugin Portal Dependency Confusion Enables Injection of Malicious Plugins into Android and Java Build Systems
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against the Gradle Plugin Portal where internal Gradle plugin names colliding with public portal plugin names caused Android Studio and Gradle build systems to automatically download and execute attacker-controlled malicious plugins during application builds.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Android Resource XML Files Alter Permission Declarations Invisible to Developers
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Android application resource XML files where hidden Unicode characters alter permission declarations and intent filter configurations, causing Android applications to request different permissions from what developers see in the manifest during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Unity Asset Store Package Injects Obfuscated Credential-Stealing Code into Game Development Projects
description: Security researchers documented malicious Unity Asset Store packages that injected obfuscated credential-stealing code into game development projects, targeting Unity developers by distributing malware through the official Unity Asset Store marketplace that serves over 750,000 game developers worldwide.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Dart Package on pub.dev Typosquats Flutter HTTP Client Library Targeting Mobile App Developers
description: A malicious Dart package published on pub.dev typosquatted the popular Flutter HTTP client library, targeting developers building Flutter mobile applications for iOS and Android by injecting data-exfiltration code into the HTTP networking layer of compromised applications.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Terraform Provider Distributed via Compromised Registry Token Exfiltrates Cloud Infrastructure Credentials
description: A malicious Terraform provider distributed through a compromised registry publishing token exfiltrated cloud infrastructure credentials including AWS access keys, Azure service principal secrets, and Google Cloud service account keys from Terraform state files and environment variables during infrastructure provisioning operations.
