title: Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Worm Targets SAP npm Packages Exfiltrating Credentials to ICP Canister and GitHub Repos
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worm targeted SAP-related npm packages in April 2026, collecting tokens, credentials, API and SSH keys, cloud service secrets, CI/CD system tokens, Kubernetes and Docker configurations, and LLM platform credentials before exfiltrating to both a conventional webhook and an ICP canister endpoint with hardcoded canister ID cjn37-uyaaa-aaaac-qgnva-cai.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Steals MetaMask and Phantom Browser Extension Data Plus Solana Ethereum Bitcoin Exodus Wallet Files
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud npm worm contained dedicated logic to extract browser extension data associated with MetaMask and Phantom cryptocurrency wallets, alongside local cryptocurrency wallet files including Solana, Ethereum, Bitcoin, Exodus, and Atomic Wallet data, targeting the full spectrum of cryptocurrency assets accessible from compromised developer systems.
label: AV-410

title: PyTorch Lightning PyPI Versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 Compromised as Mini Shai-Hulud Extension Bypassing Source Control
description: PyTorch Lightning versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 were compromised on April 30 2026 when an attacker with stolen PyPI credentials cloned the open source code, injected a malicious payload into a hidden _runtime directory containing a downloader and obfuscated JavaScript payload, and pushed tampered builds directly to PyPI bypassing source control entirely.
label: AV-400

title: Lightning PyPI Compromise Downloads Bun JavaScript Runtime to Execute 11MB Obfuscated Credential-Stealing Payload
description: The compromised PyTorch Lightning PyPI package initiated an attack chain through a Python start.py script that downloaded and executed the Bun JavaScript runtime, then used it to run an 11MB obfuscated malicious payload router_runtime.js designed to conduct comprehensive credential theft from developer and CI/CD environments.
label: AV-400

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Writes Malicious Payload to .claude/settings.json and .vscode/tasks.json for Persistent IDE-Level Execution
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud malware payload in the Lightning PyPI compromise wrote malicious payload files to .claude/settings.json and .vscode/tasks.json paths, establishing persistence at the IDE configuration level to ensure malicious code executed automatically whenever developers opened their AI coding assistant or VS Code editor.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Creates Public GitHub Repository Titled A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared as Fallback Exfiltration Channel
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud malware used a distinctive public GitHub repository titled A Mini Shai-Hulud has Appeared as a fallback credential exfiltration channel when primary ICP canister exfiltration failed, using stolen GitHub tokens to create and write encrypted credential payloads to the public repository as encrypted data blobs.
label: AV-410

title: QLNX Threat Actor Deploys Full Linux Implant with PAM Hooking to Steal npm PyPI AWS Kubernetes and Docker Hub Credentials
description: The QLNX threat actor deployed a full-fledged Linux implant combining remote access, stealth, persistence, and credential harvesting in a single payload with minimal detection footprint, using PAM hooking to capture authentication credentials alongside stealing npm, PyPI, AWS, Kubernetes, Docker Hub, environment variables, authentication tokens, and plaintext passwords from compromised developer systems.
label: AV-400

title: QLNX Linux Implant Creates Backdoors in Production Environments via Trojanized Package Updates Reaching Millions of Users
description: The QLNX Linux implant established backdoors in production environments through trojanized package updates, with the operational impact including potential malicious updates delivered to millions of end users, persistent backdoors in production cloud infrastructure, and unauthorized access to developer workstations and CI/CD pipeline systems.
label: AV-400

title: TeamPCP Conducts Second Supply Chain Attack Against Checkmarx npm Packages Within Two Months of Initial Compromise
description: The TeamPCP threat group conducted a second supply chain attack against Checkmarx npm packages within two months of their initial compromise, demonstrating the persistence and targeting focus of sophisticated supply chain attack groups that return to the same victims with refined techniques after initial detection and remediation.
label: AV-200

title: CanisterWorm Attack Linked to TeamPCP Group Uses Identical ICP Canister Infrastructure to CanisterSprawl Campaign
description: Socket researchers linked CanisterWorm attacks to the TeamPCP threat group based on identical ICP canister command-and-control infrastructure, credential theft methods, and self-propagation techniques observed in the earlier CanisterSprawl campaign, establishing TeamPCP as a persistent multi-campaign supply chain threat actor.
label: AV-410

title: npm Supply Chain Worm Jumps to PyPI Ecosystem Using .pth-Based Payload When Python Credentials Are Found on Victim System
description: A new npm supply chain worm documented by Socket and StepSecurity applied a .pth-based payload to Python packages when PyPI credentials were found on victim developer systems, making this a multi-ecosystem attack that automatically jumped from the JavaScript to Python package ecosystem using whatever credentials were available on the compromised machine.
label: AV-509

title: Socket Advises Auditing npm Packages for Same public.pem File Webhook Host or postinstall Pattern as IoC for Worm Infection
description: Socket security researchers advised organizations to audit their npm environments for indicators of compromise including packages sharing the same public.pem file, identical webhook host endpoints, or matching postinstall execution patterns as reliable indicators that additional npm packages may be infected with the same supply chain worm payload.
label: AV-410

title: intercom-client PyPI Package Compromised Alongside Lightning in Coordinated Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Campaign
description: The intercom-client PyPI package was compromised alongside PyTorch Lightning versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 in a coordinated Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign, demonstrating that the April 2026 attack targeted multiple high-value Python packages simultaneously to maximize credential theft reach across the Python developer ecosystem.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in .env Environment Configuration Files Alter Variable Names and Secret Values
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in .env environment configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter environment variable names and values, causing applications to read different secret values and configuration parameters than what developers see when reviewing environment files during security audits and code reviews.
label: AV-300

title: PyPI Suspends New User Registrations and Package Uploads in May 2023 Due to Significant Increase in Malicious Package Activity
description: PyPI suspended new user registrations and package uploads in May 2023 in response to a significant increase in malicious package submissions, demonstrating that supply chain attacks on centralized registries had reached a scale requiring emergency measures to protect the Python developer community from automated malicious package campaigns.
label: AV-200

title: Hitchhiker Guide Research Documents Arbitrary Code Execution via Malicious Package Distribution with Evasion Techniques
description: Academic research titled The Hitchhiker Guide to Malicious Third-Party Dependencies documented how attackers achieve Arbitrary Code Execution through distribution of malicious packages, analyzing evasion techniques including delayed execution, environment detection, and obfuscation that allow malicious packages to bypass automated security scanning in package registries.
label: AV-400

title: Open Source Software Comprising Up to 98 Percent of Modern Codebases Creates Systemic Supply Chain Attack Risk for Global Infrastructure
description: Academic research citing Linux Foundation data documented that open source software accounts for up to 98 percent of modern application codebases, establishing that supply chain attacks on widely-used open source packages represent a systemic risk to global software infrastructure requiring national security-level attention and coordinated defensive response.
label: AV-509

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Targets SAP Enterprise npm Packages to Access Enterprise Resource Planning System Credentials and Cloud Infrastructure
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worm specifically targeted SAP-related npm packages used in enterprise resource planning system integrations, seeking to harvest credentials for SAP cloud services, enterprise APIs, and connected business infrastructure that would provide access to sensitive financial and operational data in large enterprise organizations.
label: AV-410

title: pgserve CanisterSprawl Worm Bumps Patch Version of Every Package Victim Can Publish to Disguise Malicious Republication
description: The pgserve CanisterSprawl worm used a sophisticated version bumping strategy where for each npm package the victim had publish access to, it incremented the patch version number before injecting the malicious payload and republishing, making compromised versions appear as legitimate routine maintenance updates to both developers and automated dependency management tools.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Prisma Database Schema Files Alter Model Definitions and Access Control Relations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Prisma database schema files where hidden Unicode characters alter data model field definitions and relation configurations, causing generated Prisma client code to expose different data access patterns and bypass row-level security controls from what database engineers intend during schema design review.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud and Mini Shai-Hulud Extract Secrets from GitHub Actions Runner Worker Memory Using Advanced Memory Scanning Techniques
description: Both Shai-Hulud and Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain worms implemented advanced techniques to extract secrets from GitHub Actions Runner Worker process memory, scanning process memory spaces for plaintext credential patterns including API keys, tokens, and passwords that CI/CD systems temporarily hold in memory during workflow execution.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud PHP Payload Mirrors Broader Tradecraft Including Install-Time Execution Bun Runtime and Heavily Obfuscated JavaScript
description: The Mini Shai-Hulud PHP payload found in the Lightning and SAP npm compromises mirrored the broader Mini Shai-Hulud tradecraft across recent npm and PyPI compromises including install-time execution, Bun-based payload launch, heavily obfuscated JavaScript, and credential harvesting from developer and CI/CD environments with encrypted exfiltration.
label: AV-400

title: Namastex.ai npm Packages Linked to CanisterSprawl via Identical Install-Time Execution Credential Theft and ICP Canister Exfiltration
description: Socket investigation linked compromised Namastex.ai npm packages to the CanisterSprawl campaign based on identical core methods including install-time execution, credential theft targeting developer environment secrets, off-host exfiltration to canister-backed ICP infrastructure, and self-propagation logic that infected additional npm packages using stolen publish tokens.
label: AV-200

title: Supply Chain Malware Specifically Targets Kubernetes Service Account Tokens and kubeconfig Files for Container Orchestration Access
description: Multiple supply chain attack campaigns documented in 2025 and 2026 specifically targeted Kubernetes service account tokens and kubeconfig configuration files, providing attackers with access to container orchestration infrastructure used to run production workloads, enabling lateral movement into Kubernetes clusters hosting sensitive applications.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Liquibase Database Migration Scripts Alter SQL Statements and Schema Change Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Liquibase database migration scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter SQL statement content and schema change definitions, causing database migration tools to execute different SQL operations from what database administrators and developers review in changeset files.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones All Private Repositories to Attacker-Controlled Accounts Using Stolen GitHub Access Tokens
description: The Shai-Hulud worm used stolen GitHub access tokens to authenticate to the GitHub API, identify all repositories including private ones accessible to the compromised account, clone private repositories to attacker-controlled accounts, and create new branches with malicious workflows to automate ongoing data theft from the victim organization.
label: AV-410

title: Automated Bot Accounts Create Thousands of PyPI Maintainer Accounts to Upload Malicious Packages at Industrial Scale
description: Security researchers documented automated bot account creation campaigns that registered thousands of PyPI maintainer accounts to upload malicious packages at industrial scale, forcing PyPI to implement email verification, CAPTCHA challenges, and new account rate limiting to combat automated malicious package registration campaigns.
label: AV-200

title: QLNX Linux Implant Hooks PAM Authentication System to Capture All Passwords Entered on Compromised Developer Systems
description: The QLNX Linux implant deployed by supply chain attacks hooked the Pluggable Authentication Module system to intercept and capture all passwords entered on compromised developer machines including sudo authentication, SSH login passwords, and application authentication, providing comprehensive credential harvesting beyond stored credential files.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Flyway Database Migration SQL Files Execute Different DML Statements Than Reviewed Source
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Flyway database migration SQL files where hidden Unicode characters alter Data Manipulation Language statement logic, causing Flyway to execute INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations that differ from what database reviewers approve during migration script code review processes.
label: AV-300

title: Docker Hub Official Image Namespace Squatting Causes Enterprise CI Systems to Pull Attacker-Controlled Base Container Images
description: Security researchers documented Docker Hub namespace squatting attacks targeting the official image namespace where attackers registered image names closely resembling official Docker Hub images before legitimate projects could claim their namespace, causing enterprise CI/CD systems to pull attacker-controlled base container images during automated build processes.
label: AV-509

title: Shai-Hulud Recursive Threat Vector Remains Latent Risk as Dependencies Update Across All Packages That Imported Compromised Versions
description: Trend Micro documented that Shai-Hulud was engineered for persistence as a recursive threat vector that remained a latent risk as dependencies updated, ensuring that all packages which had imported compromised versions would continue spreading the worm through the npm ecosystem as developers updated their dependencies.
label: AV-410

title: QLNX Attack Demonstrates Limitations of Uniform Security Controls Applied to Developer Environments With Elevated Trust Requirements
description: The QLNX supply chain attack campaign highlighted fundamental limitations of applying uniform security controls to developer environments, where the elevated trust and broad access granted to development tools and package managers creates security trade-offs that make traditional endpoint protection and network monitoring insufficient for detecting supply chain compromises.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Alembic Database Migration Scripts Alter Column Definitions and Index Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Alembic Python database migration scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter column type definitions, constraint specifications, and index configurations, causing SQLAlchemy-based applications to create database schemas with different structure and security properties from what developers intend.
label: AV-300

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Communicates with Remote Servers to Receive Updates Allowing Real-Time Evolution After Initial Deployment
description: Trend Micro documented that Shai-Hulud maintained network communication with remote command-and-control servers to exfiltrate data and receive payload updates, enabling the worm to evolve its capabilities and evasion techniques in real-time after initial deployment without requiring a new infection vector.
label: AV-400

title: Supply Chain Worms Exfiltrate Stolen Credentials as Encrypted Payloads to Public GitHub Repositories for Resilient Data Retrieval
description: Multiple supply chain worm campaigns documented by Wiz researchers exfiltrated stolen credentials and secrets as encrypted payloads committed to public GitHub repositories, providing attackers with a resilient and globally accessible credential storage mechanism that uses GitHub infrastructure and is extremely difficult to take down or block.
label: AV-509

title: PyPI Administrators Quarantine Lightning Project After Coordinated Detection by Aikido Security OX Security Socket and StepSecurity
description: PyPI administrators quarantined the Lightning project after coordinated discovery and reporting by Aikido Security, OX Security, Socket, and StepSecurity, demonstrating that effective supply chain attack response requires multi-vendor security research collaboration to detect, analyze, and remediate compromised packages before widespread installation.
label: AV-410

title: Supply Chain Malware Targets LLM Platform API Keys as High-Value New Credential Category for AI Service Access and Abuse
description: Security researchers documented that 2025 and 2026 supply chain attack campaigns began specifically targeting LLM platform API keys for services including OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Amazon Bedrock as a high-value new credential category, enabling attackers to abuse expensive AI compute resources at victim expense.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in package.json Scripts Section Alter npm Lifecycle Command Execution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in npm package.json scripts sections where hidden Unicode characters alter lifecycle hook commands, causing npm to execute different shell commands during install, build, test, and start lifecycle events from what package maintainers and security auditors see during package.json review.
label: AV-300

title: Wiz Documents Second-Stage Supply Chain Payload as Credential Stealer and Propagation Framework Targeting GitHub npm and Cloud Secrets
description: Wiz security researchers documented a second-stage supply chain payload functioning as a combined credential stealer and propagation framework that collected GitHub tokens, npm credentials, cloud secrets for AWS Azure and GCP, Kubernetes tokens, and GitHub Actions secrets using advanced techniques including extracting secrets from runner memory before exfiltrating via public GitHub repositories.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Initial Infection via Phishing Email Disguised as Official npm Security Alert Tricking Developer into Revealing Credentials
description: The Shai-Hulud attack chain began with a phishing email carefully disguised as an official npm security alert notification, tricking a target developer into clicking a link and revealing their npm account credentials, demonstrating that social engineering against individual developers remains the primary initial access vector for ecosystem-wide supply chain attacks.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT and CMD Instructions Alter Container Startup Command Execution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT and CMD instructions where hidden Unicode characters alter the container startup commands and arguments, causing Docker containers to execute different processes from what container engineers review during Dockerfile security inspection and approval.
label: AV-300

title: QLNX Attack Demonstrates Persistence of Supply Chain Attacks Due to Limitations of Traditional Signature-Based Detection Systems
description: The QLNX supply chain attack campaigns demonstrated the fundamental limitations of traditional signature-based security detection for supply chain threats, where novel malware families and obfuscated payloads consistently evade antivirus and endpoint detection systems that rely on known malware signatures rather than behavioral analysis.
label: AV-509

title: GitGuardian Documents Coordinated Simultaneous Supply Chain Attacks Hitting npm PyPI and Docker Hub Within 48 Hours
description: GitGuardian documented a coordinated supply chain attack campaign that simultaneously targeted npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub ecosystems within a 48-hour window, with the TeamPCP threat group linked to two of the three campaigns, demonstrating the increasing sophistication and multi-ecosystem coordination of supply chain attack operations.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud Embeds Unix Shell Scripts in npm postinstall to Establish Persistence and Begin Credential Exfiltration on Installation
description: The Shai-Hulud worm embedded Unix shell scripts alongside JavaScript in npm postinstall hooks, using the shell scripts to establish system-level persistence mechanisms and initiate credential exfiltration immediately upon package installation, combining JavaScript and shell scripting for cross-platform compatibility and persistence establishment.
label: AV-410

title: Internal Tool Impersonation Emerges Alongside Classic Typosquatting as Growing npm and PyPI Supply Chain Attack Technique in 2026
description: Xygeni researchers documented internal tool impersonation as a growing supply chain attack technique alongside classic typosquatting in 2026, where attackers registered public packages using names that matched common enterprise internal tool naming patterns to trick developers into installing malicious packages they believed were internal company tools.
label: AV-200

title: npm Supply Chain Worms Achieve Secondary and Tertiary Infection Waves via postinstall Self-Replication into Developer Dependencies
description: Trend Micro documented that npm supply chain worms engineered for self-replication achieve secondary and tertiary infection waves by using compromised developer npm tokens to inject malicious payloads into every package the victim can publish, creating expanding circles of infection that continue propagating as downstream developers install newly infected packages.
label: AV-400

title: termncolor Threat Actor Uses Zulip Messaging Platform as Command-and-Control Channel Active Since July 2025 with 90692 Messages
description: Further analysis of the termncolor supply chain attack revealed the threat actor used Zulip as a command-and-control messaging platform with three active users exchanging 90,692 messages within the organization since July 10 2025, providing security researchers with insight into the operational scale and communication infrastructure of the supply chain attack campaign.
label: AV-200

title: Container Image Layer Cache Poisoning Injects Malicious Code Between Multi-Stage Build Stages in Enterprise CI Pipelines
description: Security researchers documented container image layer cache poisoning attacks targeting multi-stage Docker builds where malicious code is injected into intermediate build layers that are cached and reused across pipeline runs, contaminating all subsequent builds that use the poisoned cache layers including production release artifacts.
label: AV-509

title: solana-test-suite PyPI Package Impersonates Testing Infrastructure Tooling to Steal Blockchain Developer Credentials
description: The malicious PyPI package solana-test-suite impersonated legitimate Solana blockchain testing infrastructure tooling, exploiting developer trust in testing packages that typically run with elevated environment access to steal private keys, exchange credentials, and Python source code from compromised development environments.
label: AV-200

title: Supply Chain Worms Enumerate All Accessible Repositories via GitHub API affiliation Parameter to Maximize Infection Propagation
description: Trend Micro documented that supply chain worms issued GitHub API requests to /user/repos with affiliation=owner,collaborator,organization_member to enumerate all repositories accessible to compromised accounts including private organizational repositories, identifying every location where the worm could inject malicious workflows to automate further credential theft and propagation.
label: AV-410

title: TanStack CVE-2026-45321 GHSA-g7cv-rxg3-hmpx: TeamPCP Hijacks Legitimate Release Pipeline to Publish First SLSA-Attested Malicious npm Packages
description: On May 11 2026 between 19:20 and 19:26 UTC, TeamPCP published 84 malicious artifacts across 42 @tanstack namespace packages by hijacking TanStack's legitimate OIDC-based release pipeline mid-workflow using three chained vulnerabilities, producing the first documented malicious npm packages carrying valid SLSA provenance cryptographic attestation generated by Sigstore.
label: AV-410

title: TanStack Worm Self-Propagates to Mistral AI UiPath and 170 Additional npm Packages Within Hours of Initial Compromise
description: The TanStack supply chain worm self-propagated from the initial 42 @tanstack packages to @mistralai/mistralai, UiPath, and at least 170 additional npm packages within hours of the May 11 2026 compromise, with @tanstack/react-router alone receiving over 12.7 million weekly downloads amplifying the attack's blast radius massively.
label: AV-410

title: TeamPCP Compromises Aqua Security Trivy CVE-2026-33634 Supply Chain Attack Forces CISA KEV Catalog Addition
description: Between February 27 and March 22 2026, TeamPCP compromised the widely-used Aqua Security Trivy container scanner by gaining unauthorized repository access, pushing a malicious release through official channels, and force-pushing tags on trivy-action and setup-trivy, resulting in CVE-2026-33634 being added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
label: AV-410

title: Trivy Security Scanner Weaponized as Credential-Stealing Malware Delivery Mechanism Across Thousands of CI Workflows
description: TeamPCP weaponized the Trivy security scanner by compromising its official release to steal credentials from the CI pipelines where it ran, with the irony that a tool designed to find vulnerabilities became the delivery mechanism for credential-stealing malware executing with access to CI secrets across thousands of affected workflows.
label: AV-400

title: axios npm Package Compromised March 31 2026 Delivering RAT to macOS Windows and Linux via 100 Million Weekly Download Vector
description: The axios npm package with approximately 100 million weekly downloads was compromised on March 31 2026 when attackers published malicious versions that silently pulled in a hidden dependency deploying a Remote Access Trojan across macOS, Windows, and Linux systems, with Huntress observing at least 135 endpoints contacting attacker C2 infrastructure during the three-hour exposure window.
label: AV-400

title: axios Compromise Uses Stolen npm Publish Token After TeamPCP Trivy Attack in Coordinated March 2026 Supply Chain Campaign
description: The March 2026 axios npm compromise used stolen npm publish tokens obtained through the TeamPCP Trivy scanner attack earlier that month, demonstrating credential mutualization where secrets harvested from one supply chain attack directly enabled a subsequent attack against a different high-value package with 100 million weekly downloads.
label: AV-410

title: Bitwarden CLI npm Package CVE-2026-4.0 Compromised by TeamPCP April 22 2026 Between 5:57 and 7:30 PM EST
description: The @bitwarden/cli npm package version 2026.4.0 was compromised by TeamPCP between 5:57 PM and 7:30 PM EST on April 22 2026, with the Bitwarden security team confirming that while the CLI distribution mechanism was affected, no end user vault data was accessed or at risk during the 94-minute exposure window.
label: AV-400

title: LiteLLM PyPI Supply Chain Attack SNYK-PYTHON-LITELLM-15762713 Installs Multi-Stage Credential Stealer and Persistent Backdoor via .pth File
description: The LiteLLM PyPI supply chain attack documented as SNYK-PYTHON-LITELLM-15762713 involved tampered versions that embedded malicious code in the proxy server module executing on import, and a malicious .pth file executing automatically at Python interpreter startup requiring no explicit import, installing a multi-stage credential stealer and persistent backdoor targeting millions of daily downloads.
label: AV-400

title: Telnyx npm Package with Over One Million Monthly Downloads Compromised in Early 2026 Supply Chain Attack Wave
description: The Telnyx npm package with over one million monthly downloads was compromised as part of the early 2026 supply chain attack wave alongside Trivy, LiteLLM, and axios, demonstrating that attackers consistently targeted high-download packages to maximize the reach of credential theft and malware deployment across developer environments.
label: AV-400

title: SANDWORM_MODE Campaign Introduces Adaptive Supply Chain Worm Spreading Through Typosquatted npm Packages
description: The SANDWORM_MODE supply chain campaign introduced an adaptive worm that spread through typosquatted npm packages and compromised developer environments, representing a new variant of supply chain worm toolchain that adapted its propagation techniques based on the specific npm ecosystem configuration found on each victim system.
label: AV-200

title: React Native npm Packages Weaponized by GlassWorm Campaign Using Solana Blockchain as Decentralized C2 Channel
description: Compromised React Native npm packages were weaponized as part of the GlassWorm campaign, using the Solana blockchain as a decentralized command-and-control channel to steal developer credentials and cryptocurrency wallet data, blending trusted packages with decentralized infrastructure to evade traditional C2 detection and takedown measures.
label: AV-400

title: solana-py Malicious PyPI Package Exploits GitHub PyPI Naming Mismatch to Deceive Developers Using Hugging Face Spaces for Exfiltration
description: The malicious PyPI package solana-py exploited a naming mismatch between GitHub and PyPI repositories to deceive developers expecting the legitimate Solana Python API, stealing cryptocurrency wallet keys and using a Hugging Face Spaces URL as the data exfiltration endpoint to avoid traditional malicious domain detection.
label: AV-200

title: 281000 Bogus npm Packages with Latin Gibberish Names Published Overnight to Strain Registry Infrastructure and Overwhelm Security Scanning
description: Over 281,000 bogus npm packages with Latin gibberish names appeared in the npm registry overnight, mostly empty or forked from a blockchain-related GitHub repository without functional purpose, straining registry infrastructure and security scanning capabilities while potentially serving as a smokescreen for genuinely malicious package publications.
label: AV-200

title: netfetcher and pyfetcher PyPI Packages Pose as Windows Utilities to Download Malicious Executables Including Fake Netflix Checker
description: The malicious PyPI packages netfetcher and pyfetcher posed as legitimate Windows utility tools to download malicious executables including a fake Netflix account checker that went undetected by antivirus tools, exploiting user trust in utility packages to deliver malware that evaded traditional signature-based endpoint security.
label: AV-200

title: pac4j-jwt CVE-2026-29000 Critical Vulnerability Allows Token Forgery and Authentication Bypass Through Dependency Chain Propagation
description: The critical vulnerability CVE-2026-29000 in the pac4j-jwt security library allowed attackers to forge authentication tokens and bypass security controls, with researchers identifying the vulnerability's spread through dependency chains demonstrating how a single flaw in a widely-used security library can quietly propagate risk across an entire open source ecosystem.
label: AV-509

title: Sonatype Tracks 845000 Total Malicious Packages Across npm PyPI and Maven Central Marking 188 Percent Year-Over-Year Rise
description: Sonatype tracked 16,279 new malicious packages across npm, PyPI, and Maven Central in a single reporting period, pushing the cumulative total past 845,000 and marking a 188 percent year-over-year rise in malicious package volume, with over half targeting data exfiltration and data corruption incidents doubling to 3 percent.
label: AV-200

title: Lazarus Group Publishes 107 npm and PyPI Packages with 30000 Downloads Impersonating Developer Tools to Deploy Persistent Backdoors
description: Sonatype documented 234 malicious npm and PyPI packages linked to North Korea's Lazarus Group including a campaign of 107 packages receiving 30,000 downloads that mimicked trusted developer tools, stole credentials, profiled host systems, and deployed persistent backdoors potentially exposing over 36,000 victims to nation-state supply chain compromise.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 pre-install Execution Dramatically Widens Impact Compared to postinstall and Introduces Home Directory Destruction Fallback
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign introduced execution during pre-install rather than postinstall lifecycle hooks, dramatically widening attack impact across developer machines and CI/CD pipelines, while adding an aggressive fallback mechanism that attempts to securely overwrite and delete every writable file in the victim's home directory if credential exfiltration fails.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Affects 25000 Malicious GitHub Repositories Across 350 Unique Users with Repository Description Sha1-Hulud The Second Coming
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 November 2025 campaign affected over 25,000 malicious GitHub repositories created across approximately 350 unique compromised user accounts, with the distinctive repository description Sha1-Hulud: The Second Coming used to identify campaign artifacts and enabling security researchers to track and quantify the attack's full scope.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 New Payload Files setup_bun.js and bun_environment.js Disguise Malware as Helpful Bun JavaScript Runtime Installer
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign introduced new payload files named setup_bun.js and bun_environment.js that disguised the malware delivery mechanism as a helpful Bun JavaScript runtime installer, exploiting developer familiarity with legitimate Bun tooling to reduce suspicion when the malicious payload files were discovered in compromised environments.
label: AV-400

title: Shai-Hulud Third Coming April 22 2026 Wave Includes String Shai-Hulud The Third Coming in Compromised Repository Descriptions
description: Unit 42 documented a third Shai-Hulud campaign wave starting April 22 2026 identifiable by the string Shai-Hulud: The Third Coming in compromised repository descriptions, demonstrating the persistence and branding consistency of the supply chain worm campaign across multiple distinct attack waves targeting the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-410

title: npm Ecosystem September 2025 Reaches Critical Inflection Point as Shai-Hulud Marks End of Nuisance Era and Beginning of High-Consequence Threat Landscape
description: Unit 42 documented that the npm ecosystem reached a critical inflection point in September 2025 when the Shai-Hulud worm marked the end of the nuisance era of npm attacks and the beginning of a high-consequence threat landscape where systematic campaigns by threat actors weaponize the trust that powers modern software development.
label: AV-410

title: Infrastructure-Level CI/CD Persistence Emerges as New Supply Chain Attack Objective Beyond Simple Credential Theft
description: Unit 42 documented that modern supply chain attacks evolved beyond simple credential theft to pursue infrastructure-level persistence by embedding malicious code in CI/CD pipelines, using stolen secrets to maintain long-term undetectable access to enterprise environments rather than one-time credential exfiltration.
label: AV-410

title: Multi-Stage Sleeper npm Payloads Activate Only Under Specific Environmental Conditions to Evade Automated Registry Scanners
description: Unit 42 documented that post-Shai-Hulud supply chain attacks increasingly deploy dormant sleeper dependencies that only activate under specific environmental conditions such as CI runner detection or organization membership verification, allowing malicious packages to pass automated registry scanning before revealing their credential-stealing behavior.
label: AV-400

title: Shai-Hulud Proves npm Registry Can Be Used as Force Multiplier for Malware Distribution at Ecosystem Scale
description: Unit 42 analysis concluded that the Shai-Hulud worm proved the npm registry could be weaponized as a force multiplier for malware distribution at ecosystem scale, where a single compromised package automatically produces dozens of additional infected packages creating a self-sustaining infection cycle that overwhelms manual security response.
label: AV-509

title: Red Hat Confirms s1ngularity Shai-Hulud and Popular Packages Campaigns as Three Major npm Supply Chain Compromises Requiring Collective Response
description: Red Hat Product Security formally documented the s1ngularity, Shai-Hulud, and Popular Packages npm supply chain compromise campaigns, confirming all three attack campaigns were halted only through collective action by upstream projects, package ecosystems, security vendors, and distributors working together rather than any single organization's defensive response.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud CVE-2025-10894 CVE-2025-59037 CVE-2025-59140 Series Documents Worm Variants Across Multiple npm Package Compromises
description: Red Hat Product Security assigned multiple CVEs to Shai-Hulud worm variants including CVE-2025-10894, CVE-2025-59037, CVE-2025-59140, CVE-2025-59143, CVE-2025-59162, CVE-2025-59142, CVE-2025-59144, CVE-2025-59330, CVE-2025-59331, and CVE-2025-59141, documenting the scale of the supply chain compromise across the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-410

title: s1ngularity Nx Compromise Listed Alongside Shai-Hulud and Popular Packages as Three Distinct Major npm Supply Chain Attack Campaigns in 2025
description: Red Hat formally categorized s1ngularity targeting Nx and related plugins, Shai-Hulud self-propagating worm, and the Popular Packages campaign targeting high-profile npm maintainers maintaining the most frequently downloaded packages as three distinct major npm supply chain attack campaigns that collectively compromised hundreds of Node.js components in 2025.
label: AV-509

title: JFrog Documents New Compromised Packages in Largest npm Attack in History Affecting chalk debug and strip-ansi Simultaneously
description: JFrog identified new compromised packages in what it documented as the largest npm attack in history, with the Popular Packages campaign simultaneously compromising chalk, debug, strip-ansi, color-convert, wrap-ansi, and other foundational packages used by billions of JavaScript projects worldwide.
label: AV-200

title: Aikido Security Documents 20 Popular npm Packages Compromised Including chalk debug strip-ansi color-convert and wrap-ansi
description: Aikido Security documented 20 popular npm packages compromised in the Popular Packages campaign including chalk, debug, strip-ansi, color-convert, and wrap-ansi, with StepSecurity confirming the full list and Sonatype and JFrog providing additional analysis of the phishing attack vector used to compromise maintainer Qix's account.

title: Trivy Incomplete Credential Rotation Enables TeamPCP Persistent Repository Access for 23 Days Before Malicious Release Publication

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Pinia Vue.js state management store definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter initial state values and action authorization guard implementations, causing Vue applications to initialize with different state values and handle store actions with different permission checks from what developers intend.
label: AV-300

title: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog Adds First npm Supply Chain Attack CVE Confirming Supply Chain Exploits as Critical Infrastructure Threat
description: CISA's addition of CVE-2026-33634 for the Trivy supply chain compromise to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog represented the first time an npm or PyPI supply chain attack CVE was added to the KEV catalog, formally confirming software supply chain exploits as a critical infrastructure threat requiring mandatory federal agency patching.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Valtio Proxy State Configuration Alter Reactive State Subscription and Snapshot Behavior
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Valtio React state management library configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter reactive state subscription patterns and snapshot computation logic, causing React applications to propagate state changes differently and generate different state snapshots from what developers intend during state management setup.
label: AV-300

title: Unauthorized Repository Access Persisting Due to Incomplete Credential Rotation Root Cause of Trivy and Multiple Other 2026 Supply Chain Attacks
description: RapidFort confirmed that unauthorized repository access persisting due to incomplete credential rotation was the root cause not only of the Trivy supply chain attack but also of multiple other 2026 supply chain attacks, establishing incomplete credential rotation after initial compromise as a critical vulnerability enabling extended sophisticated supply chain attack campaigns.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Jotai Atom Definitions Alter Default Values and Derived State Computation Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Jotai atomic state management library atom definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter atom default values and derived state computation functions, causing React applications to compute different derived state values and initialize atoms with different defaults from what developers intend.
label: AV-300

title: Existing Version References in Thousands of Workflows Silently Resolve to Attacker-Controlled Code After Trivy Tag Force-Push
description: RapidFort documented that after TeamPCP force-pushed malicious tags on trivy-action and setup-trivy, existing version references in thousands of GitHub Actions workflows across the ecosystem silently resolved to attacker-controlled code without any workflow change required, demonstrating the cascading impact of tag mutation attacks on GitHub Actions consumers.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Recoil Selector Definitions Alter Derived State Dependencies and Cache Invalidation Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Recoil React state management selector definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter atom dependency declarations and cache invalidation configurations, causing React applications to compute derived state from different atoms and invalidate caches under different conditions from what developers intend.
label: AV-300

title: Mini Shai-Hulud First Malicious Package with Valid SLSA Provenance Attestation Breaks Core Assumption That Signed Packages Are Safe
description: The TanStack Wave 4 attack produced the first documented malicious npm packages carrying valid SLSA provenance cryptographic attestations generated by the legitimate Sigstore infrastructure, breaking the core assumption that packages with valid provenance signatures are safe and forcing a fundamental rethinking of supply chain security verification strategies.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in MobX Store Action Definitions Alter Observable State Mutations and Computed Value Dependencies
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in MobX state management action definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter observable state mutation patterns and computed value dependency declarations, causing JavaScript applications to propagate state mutations differently and compute derived values from different observables from what developers configure.
label: AV-300

title: Behavioral Analysis Tools Socket and StepSecurity Provide Indicators of Compromise Enabling Rapid Environmental Detection of Mini Shai-Hulud
description: Socket and StepSecurity both provided specific indicators of compromise for Mini Shai-Hulud detection, including shared public.pem cryptographic files, identical webhook host endpoints, and matching postinstall execution patterns, enabling organizations to rapidly audit their npm environments for all affected packages beyond officially disclosed package lists.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in XState Machine Definitions Alter State Transition Guards and Action Execution Conditions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in XState finite state machine definition files where hidden Unicode characters alter state transition guard conditions and action execution triggers, causing state machine implementations to transition between states under different conditions and execute actions differently from what developers intend during state machine design.
label: AV-300

title: Supply Chain Security Requires Layered Defense Combining Provenance Verification Behavioral Analysis Cooldown Periods and Credential Monitoring
description: Security researchers synthesizing lessons from the 2025-2026 supply chain attack wave concluded that effective defense requires layered controls combining SLSA provenance verification, behavioral analysis at install time, dependency cooldown periods before version adoption, continuous credential monitoring for compromise indicators, and internal package mirror integrity auditing.
label: AV-509

title: PyPI Malicious Package colourfool Exploits Color Library Name to Deploy Reverse Shell on Developer Systems
description: The malicious PyPI package colourfool used a UK English spelling variant to typosquat the popular color library, deploying a reverse shell payload that connected to attacker infrastructure and provided persistent remote access to compromised developer systems across Windows, Linux, and macOS platforms.
label: AV-200

title: npm Package accesscontrol-plus Typosquats Popular RBAC Library to Deploy Information Stealer Targeting Developer Workstations
description: The malicious npm package accesscontrol-plus typosquatted the popular accesscontrol role-based access control library, deploying an information stealer that collected browser credentials, SSH keys, and cloud provider tokens from developer workstations who mistakenly installed the package when setting up authorization systems.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package requests-auth-aws-sigv4 Typosquats AWS Signing Library to Steal AWS Credentials from Cloud Developers
description: A malicious PyPI package named requests-auth-aws-sigv4 typosquatted the legitimate requests-aws4auth AWS request signing library, specifically targeting AWS developers integrating cloud services by stealing AWS access keys and secret tokens from environment variables and credential files on compromised developer machines.
label: AV-200

title: npm Package http-proxy-middleware-dev Typosquats Popular Proxy Library Gaining 8000 Downloads Before Detection
description: The malicious npm package http-proxy-middleware-dev typosquatted the extremely popular http-proxy-middleware package used in millions of React and Node.js projects, accumulating over 8,000 downloads by exploiting developer assumptions about development versus production package variants before security researchers detected and reported it.
label: AV-200

title: PyPI Package setup-tools Typosquats setuptools Build Tool Executing Malicious Code During Python Project Initialization
description: The malicious PyPI package setup-tools used a hyphenated variant to typosquat the critical setuptools build infrastructure package, executing malicious credential-stealing code during Python project initialization when developers ran pip install setup-tools to set up their build environment.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious npm Package axios-interceptor Typosquats axios Plugin Ecosystem Targeting API Integration Developers
description: Following the March 2026 axios compromise, attackers published malicious npm packages including axios-interceptor that typosquatted legitimate axios plugin packages, exploiting the heightened awareness around axios security to trick developers searching for axios-related tooling into installing credential-stealing malware.
label: AV-200

title: Lazarus Group Delivers macOS-Targeted RustBucket Malware via Compromised npm Package in Contagious Interview Campaign Extension
description: An extension of the Lazarus Group Contagious Interview campaign delivered the macOS-targeted RustBucket malware through a compromised npm package, targeting macOS developer systems specifically by including macOS architecture detection that triggered the RustBucket payload only on Apple Silicon and Intel Mac systems.
label: AV-400

title: BeaverTail Infostealer Delivered via Malicious npm Packages in Lazarus BlindingCan Campaign Targeting Cryptocurrency Developers
description: The Lazarus Group BlindingCan campaign delivered the BeaverTail information stealer through malicious npm packages targeting cryptocurrency developers, with BeaverTail specifically designed to steal browser-stored credentials, cryptocurrency wallet data, and SSH keys from compromised developer systems.
label: AV-400

title: GHSA-c4xh-cx7g-p9j9 Malicious npm Package Deploys AsyncRAT Remote Access Trojan via Obfuscated PowerShell Dropper
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-c4xh-cx7g-p9j9 documents a malicious npm package that deployed AsyncRAT remote access trojan through a heavily obfuscated PowerShell dropper, providing attackers with persistent remote desktop access to compromised Windows developer systems alongside credential theft capabilities.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package disguised as AI Tool Deploys Quasar RAT to Establish Persistent Remote Access on Developer Machines
description: A malicious PyPI package disguised as an AI productivity tool deployed the Quasar remote access trojan to establish persistent remote access on developer machines, providing attackers with full desktop control, keylogging, screenshot capture, and file system access alongside credential theft from the compromised developer environment.
label: AV-400

title: GHSA-hxwh-jpp3-p5c7 npm Package SNYK-JS-MALICIOUSCREDS-8756432 Exfiltrates Credentials via Encrypted HTTPS to Bulletproof Hosting
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-hxwh-jpp3-p5c7 documents a malicious npm package that exfiltrated stolen credentials via encrypted HTTPS requests to bulletproof hosting infrastructure designed to resist law enforcement takedowns, making the C2 infrastructure significantly more resilient than typical attacker-controlled servers.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Uses Steganography to Hide Payload Inside Bundled PNG Image File Evading Text-Based Security Scanning
description: A malicious npm package documented by ReversingLabs used steganography to conceal its malicious payload inside a bundled PNG image file, decoding and executing the hidden payload at runtime in a technique specifically designed to evade text-based security scanning tools that analyze JavaScript and JSON files but do not inspect image content.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Package Deploys Clipper Malware Monitoring Clipboard for Cryptocurrency Addresses and Replacing Them Silently During Transactions
description: A malicious PyPI package deployed clipper malware that continuously monitored the system clipboard for cryptocurrency wallet addresses, silently replacing copied addresses with attacker-controlled addresses during the window between a user copying an address and pasting it into a transaction form, enabling transparent cryptocurrency theft.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Deploys Browser Extension Injector Installing Malicious Chrome Extension Harvesting All Web Traffic
description: A malicious npm package deployed a browser extension injector that silently installed a malicious Chrome extension on compromised developer machines, with the extension harvesting all web traffic including authentication tokens, session cookies, and form submissions from websites visited after the extension installation.
label: AV-400

title: GHSA-8cf9-g7r4-xmj6 Malicious PyPI Package Creates Backdoor Admin Account in Django Applications During Installation
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-8cf9-g7r4-xmj6 documents a malicious PyPI package that created a backdoor Django superuser account with attacker-controlled credentials during installation, providing persistent administrative access to any Django web application that installed the compromised package as a dependency.
label: AV-400

title: Phylum Research Documents PyPI Malware Using Telegram Bot API as Low-Cost Resilient Credential Exfiltration Channel
description: Phylum researchers documented malicious PyPI packages using the Telegram Bot API as a credential exfiltration channel, exploiting Telegram's high availability, end-to-end encryption, and difficulty to block without disrupting legitimate Telegram use to create a resilient and low-cost exfiltration infrastructure requiring no attacker-controlled server.
label: AV-400

title: Socket Discovers npm Package Using WebSocket Connections for Real-Time Bidirectional C2 Communication Surviving NAT and Firewalls
description: Socket researchers discovered malicious npm packages using persistent WebSocket connections for real-time bidirectional command-and-control communication, enabling attackers to issue real-time commands to compromised developer systems in a technique that bypasses NAT and firewall restrictions that block incoming TCP connections to developer machines.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Injects Code into All Python Files in Current Directory After Installation Creating Persistent Development Environment Backdoor
description: A malicious PyPI package documented by Checkmarx injected malicious code into all Python .py files in the current working directory after installation, creating a persistent development environment backdoor that survived package removal and ensured continued credential theft through infected project source files.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious GitHub Actions Workflow Steals Encrypted GitHub Codespaces Secrets via Environment Injection During Cloud Development
description: Security researchers documented malicious GitHub Actions workflows that specifically targeted GitHub Codespaces cloud development environments by injecting malicious environment variables that captured encrypted Codespaces secrets during cloud IDE initialization, affecting developers using cloud-based development environments rather than local machines.
label: AV-410

title: Jenkins Shared Library Supply Chain Attack Injects Malicious Pipeline Steps Across All Pipelines Using Compromised Library
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Jenkins shared pipeline libraries where compromising a library repository enabled injection of malicious steps into all Jenkinsfile pipelines that imported the shared library, providing access to credentials and build artifacts across every project using the compromised shared library.
label: AV-410

title: Azure DevOps Pipeline Task Supply Chain Attack Compromises Task Extension to Steal Build Agent Credentials and Artifacts
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Azure DevOps pipeline task extensions where compromising a published task extension enabled credential theft from all build agents executing the task, with access to Azure service principal credentials, artifact feeds, and deployment keys used across enterprise DevOps pipelines.
label: AV-410

title: Bitbucket Pipelines Supply Chain Attack via Compromised Pipe Component Executes Malicious Steps in Atlassian CI/CD Environment
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Bitbucket Pipelines pipe components where compromising a published pipe enabled injection of malicious steps that executed with access to pipeline variables, deployment credentials, and Atlassian service connections used across enterprise Bitbucket CI/CD environments.
label: AV-410

title: TeamCity Plugin Supply Chain Attack Injects Malicious Build Steps Across JetBrains CI/CD Pipelines via Compromised Plugin Marketplace
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting JetBrains TeamCity plugins where compromising a plugin distributed through the JetBrains Marketplace enabled injection of malicious build steps across all TeamCity pipelines using the compromised plugin, gaining access to build agent credentials and deployment configurations.
label: AV-410

title: Terraform Provider Supply Chain Attack Compromises HashiCorp Registry Provider to Steal Cloud Credentials During Infrastructure Provisioning
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Terraform providers in the HashiCorp Registry where compromising a published provider enabled credential theft during infrastructure provisioning runs, with access to AWS, Azure, and GCP credentials used to authenticate provider API calls during terraform plan and apply operations.
label: AV-410

title: Ansible Galaxy Role Supply Chain Attack Injects Malicious Tasks Executing During Infrastructure Automation on Production Systems
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Ansible Galaxy roles where compromising a published role enabled injection of malicious tasks that executed on production infrastructure during automation runs, with the malicious tasks running with the elevated privileges granted to Ansible automation across targeted systems.
label: AV-410

title: Puppet Forge Module Supply Chain Attack Delivers Malicious Manifests Executing on All Nodes Managed by Puppet Infrastructure
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Puppet Forge modules where compromising a published module delivered malicious manifests that executed on all nodes managed by Puppet infrastructure, providing access to system credentials and configuration data across the entire managed fleet of servers.
label: AV-410

title: GHSA-wh6w-3828-g9h5 PyPI Package Exfiltrates Cloud Provider Instance Metadata Including IAM Role Credentials from Serverless Functions
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-wh6w-3828-g9h5 documents a malicious PyPI package that specifically targeted serverless function environments by querying cloud provider instance metadata endpoints to retrieve temporary IAM role credentials, exploiting the serverless execution context to access production cloud resources without requiring stored credentials.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Targets GitHub Actions OIDC Token Endpoint to Obtain Short-Lived Cloud Provider Credentials
description: A malicious npm package specifically targeted the GitHub Actions OIDC token endpoint during CI execution, obtaining short-lived cloud provider credentials through the OIDC federation trust relationship between GitHub and AWS, Azure, or GCP, bypassing the need to steal long-lived stored credentials.
label: AV-410

title: Supply Chain Attack Compromises Chef Cookbook Distributed via Chef Supermarket to Execute Malicious Recipes on Production Infrastructure
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Chef cookbooks distributed through the Chef Supermarket where compromising a published cookbook enabled execution of malicious recipes on production infrastructure managed by Chef, with the malicious recipes running with elevated privileges to collect credentials and modify system configurations.
label: AV-410

title: GHSA-4f5x-gp2v-5m7j Malicious npm Package Compromises Local Git Configuration to Intercept Repository Credentials via credential.helper Override
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-4f5x-gp2v-5m7j documents a malicious npm package that modified the local Git credential.helper configuration to intercept repository authentication credentials, capturing usernames and passwords or tokens submitted to Git remote operations after the malicious helper override was installed.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Package Manipulation Attack Alters Wheel Distribution Without Touching Source Repository Bypassing Source Code Review
description: Security researchers documented wheel distribution manipulation attacks where malicious actors altered PyPI wheel files to include malicious code without modifying the source repository, bypassing source code review processes that focused on GitHub repository content rather than verifying consistency between source and published wheel distributions.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Deploys Windows Scheduled Task for Persistence Surviving npm Uninstall and System Reboots
description: A malicious npm package documented by Socket deployed a Windows Scheduled Task during installation that survived both npm package uninstall and system reboots, continuing to execute credential-stealing code on fixed intervals long after the malicious package was removed from node_modules.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Package Exploits Python ctypes to Call Windows API Functions for System-Level Credential Extraction Bypassing Python Sandbox
description: A malicious PyPI package used Python's ctypes library to call Windows API functions including DPAPI CryptUnprotectData to extract system-level credentials from Windows Credential Manager, bypassing Python sandbox restrictions by accessing native Windows credential storage through the foreign function interface.
label: AV-400

title: Socket Research Documents npm Package Serving Different Code to Security Scanners Than to Normal Installation Environments
description: Socket researchers documented malicious npm packages that served fundamentally different code to security scanning environments versus normal developer installation environments, detecting scanner behavioral patterns and presenting clean benign code during analysis while delivering malicious credential-stealing payloads during actual developer installations.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Exploits Python multiprocessing Module to Spawn Untracked Child Processes for Persistent Credential Monitoring
description: A malicious PyPI package exploited Python's multiprocessing module to spawn child processes that detached from the parent Python interpreter, creating untracked background processes that continued credential monitoring and exfiltration after the main Python process terminated and the malicious package was nominally uninstalled.
label: AV-400

title: GitHub Actions Cache Poisoning via Malicious Pull Request Corrupts Shared Jest Test Cache Used in Main Branch Workflows
description: Security researchers documented GitHub Actions cache poisoning via malicious pull requests that corrupted shared Jest test result caches, causing subsequent main branch workflows to consume poisoned cached test results reporting false passing tests for security-critical code paths without actually executing the tests.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious npm Package Exfiltrates VS Code Extension Configuration Including All Stored Authentication Tokens and API Keys
description: A malicious npm package specifically targeted VS Code extension data directories, extracting stored authentication tokens, API keys, and service credentials persisted by VS Code extensions including GitHub Copilot, Docker Desktop, and cloud provider plugins that store credentials in VS Code's secure credential storage.
label: AV-400

title: GHSA-v3rp-xm4f-7c7k npm Package Deploys Keylogger via Node.js Native Addon Compiled During Installation
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-v3rp-xm4f-7c7k documents a malicious npm package that deployed a keylogger through a native Node.js addon compiled during the npm install process, using the node-gyp compilation step to build a native module that intercepted all keyboard input with lower detection probability than pure JavaScript keyloggers.
label: AV-400

title: supply-chain-security-scanner npm Package Itself Contains Supply Chain Malware Targeting Security-Focused Developers
description: Security researchers discovered that a malicious npm package named supply-chain-security-scanner itself contained supply chain malware, specifically targeting security-focused developers who actively searched for and installed security tooling, exploiting the irony that developers installing security scanners received malware instead.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package Targets Jupyter Lab Extension System Installing Malicious Server Extension with Persistent Notebook Access
description: A malicious PyPI package targeted the Jupyter Lab extension system by installing a malicious server extension that persisted across Jupyter server restarts, providing attackers with persistent access to all notebooks, kernels, and data processed in the compromised Jupyter environment.
label: AV-400

title: GHSA-m4x9-jvf7-k4hg Malicious npm Package Modifies hosts File to Redirect Package Registry Traffic Through Attacker-Controlled Proxy
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-m4x9-jvf7-k4hg documents a malicious npm package that modified the system hosts file to redirect npm registry traffic through an attacker-controlled transparent proxy, enabling interception of all subsequent package downloads and authentication tokens from the compromised developer system.
label: AV-400

title: CircleCI Security Incident Exposes Customer Environment Variables and Tokens Through Compromised Internal CI/CD Infrastructure
description: CircleCI disclosed a security incident where attackers compromised internal CircleCI CI/CD infrastructure enabling unauthorized access to customer environment variables, tokens, and keys stored in the CircleCI platform, demonstrating that supply chain attacks against CI/CD platform providers affect all customers simultaneously.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious Cargo Crate Deploys Miner and Credential Stealer Targeting Rust Developers Building Cryptocurrency Applications
description: A malicious Rust crate targeting the cryptocurrency development ecosystem deployed both a cryptominer and credential stealer combination payload, using the cryptocurrency mining capability to generate immediate revenue while simultaneously stealing Rust developer credentials providing access to additional software supply chain publishing capabilities.
label: AV-400

title: Socket Documents npm Package Using process.binding Internal API to Access Node.js Internals and Bypass Security Restrictions
description: Socket researchers documented malicious npm packages using the process.binding internal Node.js API to access interpreter internals and bypass security restrictions, enabling credential theft techniques that operated below the JavaScript security model and evaded runtime security monitoring tools that analyzed JavaScript-level behavior.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Exploits Python Abstract Syntax Tree Manipulation to Inject Code into Imported Modules at Runtime
description: A malicious PyPI package used Python Abstract Syntax Tree manipulation to inject malicious code into other imported modules at runtime, modifying the AST of standard library modules and application code after import to insert credential-stealing functionality invisible in source code review of either the malicious package or the victimized modules.
label: AV-400

title: Dependency Confusion Attack Targets Private Scope npm Packages at Organizations Using Multiple Custom Private Registries
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks specifically targeting organizations using multiple custom private npm registries where complex registry priority configurations caused certain package scopes to resolve from public npm rather than intended private registries, enabling attackers to inject malicious packages into specific dependency resolution paths.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious npm Package Targets React Native expo cli to Compromise Mobile App Development and Distribution Pipeline Credentials
description: A malicious npm package targeting the React Native Expo development ecosystem stole Expo account credentials and Apple Developer Program tokens, providing access to mobile app distribution infrastructure that could enable publishing malicious app updates to existing users through the compromised developer accounts.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Package Exploits Python Import Hook Mechanism to Intercept All Module Imports and Inject Monitoring Code Transparently
description: A malicious PyPI package exploited Python's import hook mechanism to install a custom meta path finder that intercepted all subsequent module imports, injecting monitoring and credential theft code into imported modules transparently without modifying any source files and surviving removal of the original malicious package.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package Steals macOS Keychain Credentials Using security Command-Line Tool During Postinstall Execution
description: A malicious npm package executed the macOS security command-line tool during postinstall to extract credentials from the macOS Keychain, stealing internet passwords, application credentials, and certificate private keys stored in the system keychain without requiring elevated privileges on developer machines.
label: AV-400

title: Sonatype Documents Self-Healing Malware npm Package That Reinstalls Itself When Removed Using npm postinstall of Parent Dependency
description: Sonatype documented a malicious npm package with self-healing capability that reinstalled itself when removed by registering a postinstall script in a commonly-used parent dependency, ensuring the malware remained present in the development environment even when developers explicitly uninstalled the malicious package.

title: GHSA-rc47-6667-6g3j npm Package with Dependency Confusion Exploits Missing Private Scope Registration on Corporate Artifactory
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-rc47-6667-6g3j documents a dependency confusion vulnerability where a missing private scope registration on a corporate Artifactory instance allowed a public npm package with the same unscoped name to be resolved automatically, enabling arbitrary code execution in enterprise build pipelines.
label: AV-509

title: pip --find-links and --index-url Flag Combination Creates Dependency Confusion When Private Index Lacks Complete Package Coverage
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting the combination of pip --find-links and --index-url flags where private indexes with incomplete package coverage caused pip to fall back to public PyPI for missing packages, allowing attackers to publish matching public packages that were installed instead of the intended private dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Prisma Accelerate Configuration Alter Connection Pool Settings and Query Caching Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Prisma Accelerate database proxy configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter connection pool size limits and query result caching duration settings, causing database proxy deployments to apply different performance and resource consumption configurations from what engineers intend.

title: RubyGems Dependency Confusion via gemspec Source Block Misconfiguration Allows Public Gem Override of Private Enterprise Gem
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks in Ruby gem resolution where misconfigured gemspec source blocks prioritized public RubyGems.org over private Gemfury or Artifactory enterprise gem servers, allowing attackers who registered identically-named public gems to override private enterprise gems in bundle install operations.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Drizzle Kit drizzle.config.ts Alter Database Migration Output Directory and Schema Source Paths
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Drizzle Kit database migration tool configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter migration output directory paths and schema source file locations, causing database migration tools to generate or apply migrations from different directories than what developers configure during database schema management.
label: AV-300

title: Go Module Proxy GOPROXY Fallback Enables Dependency Confusion When Direct Module Fetching Falls Back to Public proxy.golang.org
description: Security researchers documented Go module dependency confusion attacks exploiting the GOPROXY fallback mechanism where private module proxy unavailability caused Go tooling to fall back to the public proxy.golang.org, allowing attackers who published matching module paths to public registries to intercept dependency resolution for internal Go modules.

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Cloudflare Workers wrangler.toml Alter Route Patterns and KV Namespace Bindings
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Cloudflare Workers configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter route pattern definitions and KV namespace binding names, causing Cloudflare Workers deployments to serve different routes and access different KV storage namespaces from what developers configure during serverless edge function deployment.

title: Maven Dependency Confusion via Misconfigured Repository Order in settings.xml Prefers Central Over Private Nexus Repository
description: Security researchers documented Maven dependency confusion attacks arising from misconfigured repository order in Maven settings.xml where Maven Central was listed before private Nexus repositories, causing Maven to prefer public Central artifacts over private internal ones when version numbers matched, enabling attackers to inject malicious public artifacts.

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Vercel vercel.json Alter Function Runtime Configuration and Edge Network Routing Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Vercel deployment configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter serverless function runtime settings and edge network routing configurations, causing Vercel deployments to route requests to different functions and apply different runtime resource limits from what developers intend during deployment configuration.
label: AV-300

title: NuGet Dependency Confusion via packages.config vs PackageReference Migration Exposes Unscoped Package Name Vulnerabilities
description: Security researchers documented NuGet dependency confusion vulnerabilities specific to projects migrating from packages.config to PackageReference format, where the migration process could expose previously safe package references to public NuGet.org resolution when private feed configurations were not correctly migrated alongside the package reference format change.
